collection.name: The Purple Carrot Archive
collection.notes: Images are ordered oldest-first within each capture run. Six complete Extras sheets are treated as single-page recipes. One adjacent pair contains a Fabcake Sliders cover and a Sweet Potato Lasagna method, so they are retained as two partial recipes.

recipes.title: Tomato Zucchini Fritters
recipes.subtitle: with Butter Bean Succotash & Aioli
recipes.summary: with Butter Bean Succotash & Aioli
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Shuck the corn and cut kernels off the cob. Drain and rinse the butter beans. Dice the red bell pepper(s). Peel and mince the shallots). Peel and mince the garlic. Halve and juice the lemon(s). Slice the radish(es) into thin rounds. Grate the zucchini(s) on the medium side of a box grater. Dice the tomato(es).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp (2 tsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add corn kernels and a pinch of salt and cook until tender. Add cooked corn, beans, diced pepper, minced shallot, just half the lemon juice, 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil, ¼ tsp (½ tsp) salt, and a sprinkle of pepper to a medium bowl. Mix the butter bean succotash. TIP: We will reuse this skillet in a later step.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add grated zucchini, diced tomato, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a colander. Let sit 5 to 6 minutes, and squeeze as much water out of the mixture as possible. Place mixture in a medium bowl, and add garbanzo bean flour and Alamo blend chili seasoning. Stir to combine. TIP: To remove moisture from the zucchini and tomato, use your hands or a spatula to press the mixture against the colander.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Wipe the large nonstick skillet clean, add 1 tbsp vegetable oil, and heat over medium-high heat. Scoop 6 portions of tomato zucchini fritter batter into the skillet, each about ¼ cup. Use the bottom of a measuring cup bottom to flatten the fritters. Cook fritters until browned and crisp, 3 to 5 minutes per side. Transfer tomato zucchini fritters to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: Work in batches for the 4 serving meal, adding 1 tbsp vegetable oil for the second batch.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add minced garlic, remaining lemon juice, Veganaise, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a small bowl, and stir the aioli. Divide the butter bean succotash between bowls. Top with tomato zucchini fritters. Garnish with aioli and sliced radish. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Black Pepper Tofu
recipes.subtitle: with Chinese Broccoli & Brown Rice
recipes.summary: with Chinese Broccoli & Brown Rice

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add brown rice, 1½ cups (3 cups) water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until the water is absorbed and grains are tender, 30 to 35 minutes. Fluff brown rice with a fork.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and mince the garlic, ginger, and shallots). Mince the bird's eye chile(s). Drain tofu, pat dry, and cut into 1 inch cubes. Chop scallions into 2 inch pieces. Thinly slice the celery. Roughly chop the Chinese broccoli. TIP: Make sure to wash your hands after prepping the chile(s).

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add cubed tofu and cornstarch to a large bowl, and toss. Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add cubed tofu and cook, tossing occasionally, until browned in places, 5 to 8 minutes. Transfer crispy tofu to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: If necessary, work in batches for the 4 serving meal.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return the large nonstick skillet to medium heat and add the butter. After butter is melted, add minced garlic, minced ginger, minced shallot, and 1 tsp (2 tsp) minced bird's eye chile. Cook until shallot softens, 1 to 2 minutes. In a small bowl, whisk the tamari and brown sugar until sugar dissolves. TIP: Add more minced bird's eye chile if you prefer.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add crispy tofu, chopped scallion, sliced celery, tamari mixture, and ¼ tsp (½ tsp) pepper to the skillet with the aromatics. Cook until tofu is heated through, 3 to 4 minutes. TIPS: Do not wash the skillet; we'll use it again in the next step. Add more pepper if you prefer more spice.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the brown rice between bowls and top with the black pepper tofu. Return the large nonstick skillet to high heat and add chopped broccoli and ¼ cup (½ cup) water. Sprinkle with salt, and cook until broccoli is crisp-tender, 2 to 3 minutes. Serve Chinese broccoli on the side. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Korean Tofu Tacos
recipes.subtitle: with Kimchi Slaw & Cilantro
recipes.summary: with Kimchi Slaw & Cilantro
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Peel and mince the garlic. Peel and mince 2 tsp (4 tsp) ginger. Add minced garlic, minced ginger, gochujang, peanut butter, tamari, turbinado sugar, sesame oil, and 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) warm water to a medium bowl. Whisk the gochujang sauce. TIPS: The oven will be used to warm the tortillas. You can use a toaster oven or microwave instead if you prefer. Save any remaining ginger for your own use.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Cut tofu into ½ inch cubes. Heat 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add cubed tofu and cook until crispy in places, 3 to 5 minutes. Add gochujang sauce and cook another 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add coleslaw blend, kimchi, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a large bowl. Toss the kimchi slaw to combine.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Wrap tortillas in foil and place them in the oven to warm, 4 to 5 minutes. Roughly chop the cilantro leaves and stems. Divide warm tortillas between plates and top with Korean tofu and kimchi slaw. Top the Korean tofu tacos with chopped cilantro. Tuck in! Healthy Kimchi Aside from being a convenient way to preserve vegetables, kimchi is known for its incredible health benefits. It's packed with probiotics, plus it's rich in vitamin C, beta-carotene, and fiber. These elements are essential for healthy digestion, immunity, and heart health, and scientists are continually discovering even more benefits of adding kimchi to your diet.

recipes.title: Lemon Zucchini Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Cashew Cream & Crispy Artichoke Hearts
recipes.summary: with Cashew Cream & Crispy Artichoke Hearts

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add cashews and ½ cup hot water to a small bowl, and let nuts soak for at least 10 minutes. Drain artichoke hearts and pat dry.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add artichoke hearts and cook until crispy in places, 5 to 8 minutes. Transfer crispy artichoke hearts to a plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: We will use this skillet again in a later step.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Trim zucchini and slice lengthwise into ¼ inch thick planks. Stack the zucchini planks and cut into thin strips to make noodles. TIP: You can also use a spiralizer or mandoline to make the zucchini noodles.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Zest and halve the lemon, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Peel the garlic. Add soaked cashews and the soaking water, lemon juice, peeled garlic, white miso paste, pepper to a blender. Blend on high until cashew cream is smooth.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return skillet to medium-high heat and add zucchini noodles. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and cook, tossing occasionally, until tender, 1 to 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide zucchini noodles between serving bowls. Pour cashew cream over noodles and top with crispy artichoke hearts. Sprinkle with lemon zest, hemp seeds, pumpkin seeds, and as much of the red chile flakes as you'd like. Serve with lemon wedges. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Sweet Corn Stuffed Poblanos
recipes.subtitle: with Creamed Spinach & Cilantro
recipes.summary: with Creamed Spinach & Cilantro

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Add 2 cups (4 cups) water and bouillon cube(s) to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil. Slice one side of each poblano pepper, from stem to bottom, without fully cutting peppers in half. Remove seeds and as much of the pith as possible. Peel and thinly slice the onion(s). Peel and mince the garlic. Shuck the corn and cut kernels off the cobs).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Once the broth in the saucepan is boiling, add corn kernels, polenta, sugar, just ¼ cup (½ cup) coconut milk, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Whisk until mixture comes to a boil, and cook until thickened, 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Place sliced onion in a baking dish and drizzle with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil. Top with the sliced poblano peppers, and spoon sweet corn filling into each pepper. Dollop peppers with cashew cheese and bake, uncovered, until peppers are tender, 20 to 25 minutes. TIP: You may have extra filling left over depending on the size of your peppers.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp (2 tsp) olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add just ½ cup (1 cup) coconut milk, baby spinach, a pinch of pepper. Cook until creamed spinach thickens slightly, 4 to 5 minutes. TIP: Keep any remaining coconut milk for your own use.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Divide the creamed spinach between plates and top with sweet corn stuffed poblanos and roasted onions. Sprinkle with cilantro leaves and stems. Bon appétit! Fresh Corn is Our Fave We make corn the star of this recipe, allowing its natural sweetness to really shine. It also pairs well with the poblanos, and the creamed spinach and cilantro perfectly balance things out.

recipes.title: Plantain Arepas
recipes.subtitle: with Tofu Scramble & Guacamole
recipes.summary: with Tofu Scramble & Guacamole

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel the plantain and slice into ½ inch thick rounds. Add sliced plantain to a small saucepan, cover with 1 inch water, and bring to a boil. Cook until fork-tender, 4 to 6 minutes. Drain and return plantain to the saucepan, off heat. Mash boiled plantain with a fork.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Trim, deseed, and thinly slice the mini sweet peppers. Peel the shallot, slice in half, thinly slice one half, and dice the other half. Drain the tofu, pat dry, and crumble.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add 2 tbsp vegetable oil and ground turmeric to a large skillet over medium heat. Cook until fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes. Add sliced mini sweet peppers and sliced shallot. Cook until softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add crumbled tofu, lightly browned, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer the tofu scramble to a plate. TIP: We will this skillet again in the next step.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add mashed plantain, masa harina, and ¼ tsp salt to a large bowl. Add ¼ cup water and mix dough with your hands. Divide arepa dough into 4 equal parts and shape into small, flat rounds about ½ inch thick. Wipe the large skillet clean and return it to medium heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add plantain arepas and cook until golden brown, 3 to 5 minutes per side. TIP: If dough is dry, add water-up to 2 tbsp-until you achieve the desired consistency.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Dice the tomato. Roughly chop cilantro leaves and stems. Halve the lime, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Halve the avocado, scoop flesh into a medium bowl, and mash with a fork. Add diced shallot, diced tomato, chopped cilantro, lime juice, and a pinch of salt to the bowl, and stir the guacamole.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Halve the plantain arepas crosswise and spread butter on each half. Add guacamole and tofu scramble to one half of each arepa, and top with the remaining arepa half. Serve with lime wedges and any remaining tofu scramble and guacamole. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Mushroom Cheddar Black Bean Burgers
recipes.subtitle: with Garlic Aioli & Carrot Fries
recipes.summary: with Garlic Aioli & Carrot Fries
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Peel the carrots and slice into sticks. Add sliced carrots, 2 tsp vegetable oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a baking sheet, and toss to coat. Bake carrot fries until tender and browned in spots, 18 to 22 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Thinly slice the mushrooms. Halve the onion, thinly slice one half, and mince the other half. Heat 2 tsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced mushrooms, sliced onion, and just ½ tsp fajita seasoning. Cook until mushrooms are browned in places, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer the sautéed vegetables to a bowl. running water, and wipe mushrooms clean with a damp kitchen towel. We will use this skillet again.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Drain and rinse the black beans. Peel and mince the garlic. Add just ½ cup minced onion, remaining fajita seasoning, black beans, just half the minced garlic, and flour to a large bowl. Mix with your hands, crushing the beans, until burger mix is sticky and uniform. Form into 2 patties, each about 1 inch thick. TIP: Keep any remaining minced onion for your own use.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return the large nonstick skillet to medium-high heat with 2 tbsp vegetable oil. Add black bean patties and cook until crisp and heated through, 3 to 5 minutes per side. Remove skillet from heat, top cooked black bean patties with cheddar, and cover until melted, 3 to 5 minutes. TIP: Add more oil as necessary to crisp the burgers.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add remaining minced garlic, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl. Mix the garlic aioli.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Toast the potato buns. Spread garlic aioli on the top half of each bun, and place black bean patties on the bottom halves. Top with sautéed vegetables. Serve the mushroom cheddar black bean burgers with carrot fries and garlic aioli for dipping. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Punjabi Kadhi
recipes.subtitle: with Spinach Dumplings & Mango Chutney
recipes.summary: with Spinach Dumplings & Mango Chutney
ingredients: ¼ cup Forager Project® Organic Dairy-free Cashewmilk Yogurt

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and finely dice the onion. Peel and mince the garlic and ginger. Halve, deseed, and thinly baby spinach. slice the jalapeño. Roughly chop just half the the jalapeño. TIP: Remember to wash your hands after handling

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Place a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add chopped spinach and 2 tbsp water and cook, stirring occasionally, until spinach is bright green, 1 to 2 minutes. Drain, and transfer spinach to a medium bowl. To that same bowl, add just 2 tbsp diced onion, garbanzo bean flour, yogurt, baking powder, 2 tbsp vegetable oil, and ¼ tsp salt. Stir the dumpling mixture until just combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Return the empty saucepan to medium heat with 2 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add remaining diced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger. Cook until softened, 2 to 3 minutes. Add curry powder and stir. Add coconut milk, bouillon cube, and 2z cup water, and bring sauce to a simmer. Cook until slightly thickened, 3 to 5 minutes. Once curry sauce has thickened reduce heat to low.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Drop heaping spoonfuls of dumpling mixture into the sauce; you should get 8 dumplings. Cover dumplings and gently simmer until they expand and are firm to the touch, 5 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced jalapeño, remaining baby spinach, and a pinch of salt. Cook until spinach is bright green and wilted, 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Make a small tear at the top of the brown rice bag and microwave bag for 1 minute. Divide warm brown rice between large bowls. Top with sautéed spinach and punjabi kadhi. Dollop with mango chutney. Tuck in! TIP:To reheat the rice without a microwave, add precooked brown rice and 1 tbsp water to a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Cover and steam until warmed through, 3 to 4 minutes.

recipes.title: Spinach Artichoke Grilled Cheese
recipes.subtitle: with Hot Pepper Mayo & Oregano Fries
recipes.summary: with Hot Pepper Mayo & Oregano Fries
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Cut potato into ½ inch thick sticks and transfer to a baking sheet. Add 2 tsp olive oil, oregano, and a pinch of salt and pepper, and toss. Roast until browned and crisp in places, 18 to 20 minutes. TIP: Rotate the baking sheet halfway through baking to ensure even browning.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Finely chop the pickled jalapeños. Add chopped jalapeños, just half the Vegenaise, and a pinch of pepper to a small bowl. Stir the hot pepper mayo. TIP: If you're sensitive to spice, omit or use less pickled jalapeño.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Drain and roughly chop ½ cup artichoke hearts. Mince the garlic. Add 1 tsp olive oil to a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add chopped artichoke hearts and cook until crispy in places, 5 to 6 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook until aromatic, about 1 minute. Add baby spinach, stir to combine, and transfer spinach artichoke mixture to a bowl. Wipe the skillet clean and set aside, off heat. TIPS: Keep the remaining artichoke hearts for your own use. We will use this skillet again.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: To assemble the sandwiches, place 2 slices of sourdough bread on a flat surface and spread just ½ tbsp Vegenaise on each slice. Flip slices over and spread hot pepper mayo on the other side. Add spinach artichoke mixture and mozzarella, and top with remaining bread slices to close the sandwiches. Spread remaining Vegenaise on top of each sandwich.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Place the large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add sandwiches and cook until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes per side. TIP: If the bread browns before the cheese has melted, lower the heat.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Cut the spinach artichoke grilled cheese sandwiches in half and serve with oregano fries. Enjoy!

steps.text: Add gnocchi to the large pot of boiling water and cook until they float to the surface, 3 to 5 minutes. Drain, and shake off any excess water.

recipes.title: Rajas Tacos
recipes.subtitle: with Avocado & Roasted Corn Salsa
recipes.summary: with Avocado & Roasted Corn Salsa

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add Spanish rice and 1 cup (2 cups) water to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until water is absorbed and rice is tender, 13 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Slice peppers in half and remove the stems and seeds. Cut halved peppers into ½ inch strips. Peel and slice the onions). Shuck the corn and slice kernels off the cobs). Halve the avocado(s), remove the pits), and dice the flesh. Slice the scallion(s). Halve the lime(s), juice one half (1 lime), and divide the other half (1 lime) into wedges. Roughly chop the cilantro leaves and stems. TIP: To cut kernels off the cob, break cob in half and slice down the sides with a knife. If you are grilling this recipe, remove kernels from cob after grilling.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Set the broiler to high. Add sliced pepper, sliced onion, corn kernels, 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) vegetable oil, and a pinch of salt to a baking sheet, and toss. Broil the corn salsa on the middle rack until onions are translucent and peppers and corn are softened and lightly browned, 5 to 6 minutes. TIP: All broilers are different; watch closely to ensure veggies don't burn.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Set the oven to 400°F. Add diced avocado, sliced scallion, lime juice, chopped cilantro, and salt to a medium bowl, and stir. Wrap tortillas in foil and warm in the oven, 3 to 4 minutes. TIP: If you prefer, you can use a toaster oven or microwave to heat the tortillas instead.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add Spanish rice and roasted corn salsa to the tortillas. Dollop with avocado mixture and drizzle with hot sauce. Serve the rajas tacos with lime wedges. Buen provecho! What Does Rajas Mean? Place corn in the husk, on the grill rack, and cook, turning occasionally, until it is steamed through to the center, about 15 minutes. The outside should be completely black, but the interior should be steamed and moist.

recipes.title: Pan Seared Broccolini
recipes.subtitle: with Mashed Sweet Potatoes & Coconut Turmeric Relish
recipes.summary: with Mashed Sweet Potatoes & Coconut Turmeric Relish

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the sweet potato(es). Add diced sweet potato to a small saucepan and cover with 1 inch water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes. Drain and return cooked sweet potato to the saucepan, off heat. Rinse and sort the beluga lentils, and add to a separate saucepan with 1 cup (2 cups) water. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender, 20 to 24 minutes. TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally occurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Trim, halve, and deseed the Fresno chile(s), and mince one half (1 Fresno chile). Peel and mince 2 tsp (4 tsp) ginger. Halve and juice the lemon(s). Trim 1 inch off the broccolini stems. TIP: Save the remaining ginger for your own use.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add sliced garlic and cook until lightly browned, 3 to 5 minutes. Add minced Fresno chile, minced ginger, golden raisins, toasted coconut, brown mustard seeds, and turmeric. Stir and cook until aromatic, 1 to 3 minutes. Add lemon juice and transfer the coconut turmeric relish to a medium bowl. TIPS: Add more Fresno chile if you prefer more heat, and save any remaining chile for your own use. We will use this skillet again in the next step.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Wipe the skillet clean and return to mediumhigh heat. Add 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil, ¼ cup (½ cup) water, and a pinch of salt. Add broccolini, cover, and cook until water evaporates and broccolini is tender and crispy in places, 3 to 5 minutes. Flip and cook for an additional 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add butter to the cooked sweet potatoes and mash with a fork. Taste, and add salt as necessary. Divide the mashed sweet potatoes and beluga lentils between large plates. Top with pan seared broccolini and coconut turmeric relish. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Crispy Cauliflower Pakoras
recipes.subtitle: with Red Lentil Dal & Cilantro Chutney
recipes.summary: with Red Lentil Dal & Cilantro Chutney

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the onion. Peel and mince the garlic and ginger. Chop broccoli florets and cauliflower florets into bite-size pieces. Thinly slice the mini sweet peppers. Add vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl and whisk until sugar dissolves. Add sliced mini sweet peppers and toss to combine.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Rinse and sort the lentils. Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add diced onion, just half the minced garlic, minced ginger, and a pinch of salt. 3 to 4 minutes. Cook, stirring frequently, until onion softens, TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally occurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add curry powder to the small saucepan, stir, and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add lentils, coconut milk, and ¾ cup water. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until lentils are tender and water is absorbed, 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add remaining minced garlic, garbanzo bean flour, garam masala, ½ cup cold water, and ¼ tsp salt to a large bowl and whisk the pakora batter.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add chopped broccoli florets and chopped cauliflower florets to the pakora batter and toss to coat. Heat ½ cup vegetable oil in a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, gently place coated vegetables in the skillet. Cook until lightly browned, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: Work in batches to avoid overcrowding

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the red lentil dal between bowls and top with crispy cauliflower pakoras and pickled mini sweet peppers. Drizzle with cilantro chutney. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Smoky Tofu & Sweet Potato Hash
recipes.subtitle: with Avocado
recipes.summary: with Avocado

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Dice the sweet potato. Trim, halve and thinly slice the onion. Trim and thinly slice the mini sweet peppers. Crumble the tofu. Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and thinly slice the flesh.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add diced sweet potato and cook until lightly browned, 3 to 4 minutes. Add ⅓ cup water, cover, and steam until potato is fork-tender, 3 to 4 minutes. Add sliced onion, sliced sweet peppers, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until vegetables are browned, 5 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in another large skillet over medium-high heat. Add crumbled tofu, nutritional yeast, paprika, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until tofu is golden brown, 5 to 8 minutes. Divide the sweet potato hash between plates and top with smoky tofu. Serve with sliced avocado.

recipes.title: Lemon Zucchini Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Cashew Cream & Crispy Artichoke Hearts
recipes.summary: with Cashew Cream & Crispy Artichoke Hearts

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add cashews and ½ cup hot water to a small bowl, and let nuts soak for at least 10 minutes. Drain artichoke hearts and pat dry with a clean towel.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add artichoke hearts and cook until crispy in places, 5 to 8 minutes. Transfer crispy artichoke hearts to a plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: We will use this skillet again.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Trim zucchini and slice lengthwise into ¼ inch thick planks. Stack the planks and cut into thin strips to make noodles. TIP: You can also use a spiralizer or mandoline to make the zucchini noodles.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Zest and halve the lemon, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Peel the garlic. Add soaked cashews and the soaking water, lemon juice, peeled garlic, white miso paste, pepper to a blender. Blend on high until cashew cream is smooth.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return skillet to medium-high heat and add zucchini noodles. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and cook, tossing occasionally, until tender, 1 to 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide zucchini noodles between serving bowls. Pour cashew cream over noodles and top with crispy artichoke hearts. Sprinkle with lemon zest, hemp seeds, pumpkin seeds, and as much of the red chile flakes as you'd like. Serve with lemon wedges. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Spicy Mango Tempeh
recipes.subtitle: with Toasted Almond Couscous & Lemon Arugula
recipes.summary: with Toasted Almond Couscous & Lemon Arugula

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Cut tempeh into cubes. Peel and thinly slice the shallot. Chop the parsley leaves. Zest, halve and juice the lemon.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: In a medium bowl, combine just half the lemon juice, mango chutney, chile garlic sauce, 1 tbsp olive oil, and 1 tbsp water. Add a pinch of salt and stir the spicy mango sauce. Add almonds to a small saucepan over medium heat and cook until toasted, 3 to 5 minutes. Transfer toasted almonds to a small bowl.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Return empty saucepan to high heat and add 1 cup water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, add Israeli couscous, cover, and reduce heat to low. Cook until water is absorbed, 7 to 9 minutes. Add chopped parsley, lemon zest, toasted almonds, and 2 tsp olive oil. Taste toasted almond couscous and add salt as necessary.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add cubed tempeh and cook until crispy on all sides, 8 to 10 minutes. Sprinkle tempeh with salt and add sliced shallot. Cook until shallot is softened and browned, 2 to 4 minutes. Add spicy mango sauce, toss to coat, and cook for 1 minute.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add baby arugula, remaining lemon juice, and 1 tsp olive oil to a large bowl. Toss, and sprinkle with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the lemon arugula between large bowls. Top with toasted almond couscous and spicy mango tempeh. Dig in!

recipes.title: Summer Vegetable Ciabatta
recipes.subtitle: with Cashew Cheese & Roasted Tomato Vinaigrette
recipes.summary: with Cashew Cheese & Roasted Tomato Vinaigrette
ingredients: 2 oz Treeline® Soft French-Style Cashew Cheese

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel the garlic and mince just one clove. Slice zucchini into thick rounds on a bias. Halve the mini sweet peppers and remove the seeds and stems. Peel the onion and slice into wedges. Pick and roughly chop the basil. Roughly chop the roasted tomatoes. TIP: To cut something "on a bias" means to cut it diagonally instead of crosswise.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Set broiler to high. Add sliced zucchini, halved peppers, sliced onion, 1 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a baking sheet, and toss. Broil until vegetables are tender and slightly browned, 5 to 6 minutes. TIP: All broilers are different. Watch carefully to ensure food doesn't burn.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add minced garlic, chopped tomatoes, red wine vinegar, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a sprinkle of salt and pepper to a medium sized bowl. Whisk the roasted tomato vinaigrette to combine.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Slice the ciabatta rolls in half. Broil in the oven, directly on the rack, until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes. Cut the tip off the remaining garlic clove and rub the exposed clove on the toasted ciabatta.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Spread cashew cheese on the bottom half of each toasted ciabatta roll. Pour just half of the roasted tomato vinaigrette on the broiled summer vegetables, directly on the baking sheet, and toss. Add vegetables to the toasted ciabatta and top with chopped basil. Add baby arugula to the remaining vinaigrette and toss. Serve arugula salad alongside the summer vegetable ciabatta. Dig in! Get Grillin' If you opt to make this on the grill instead, you can use skewers for the veggies. (And if you're using wooden skewers make sure to soak them in water first; we recommend reading the instructions.) We also suggest cutting the zucchini into chunks instead of planks. It'll be a lot easier and every bit as delicious.

recipes.title: Crispy Black Bean Patties
recipes.subtitle: with Avocado Spinach Salad & Lime Aioli
recipes.summary: with Avocado Spinach Salad & Lime Aioli
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Drain and rinse the black beans. Peel and mince the garlic. Peel and finely dice just ½ cup onion. Dice the cucumber. Halve the grape tomatoes. Zest, halve, and juice the lime. TIP: Save the remaining onion for your own use.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add just ¼ tsp minced garlic, just ½ tsp lime zest, just 2 tsp lime juice, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl and mix the lime aioli.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add black beans, remaining minced garlic, diced onion, garbanzo bean flour, zhoug seasoning, and ½ tsp salt to a large bowl. Mix with your hands, crushing the beans, until dough is sticky and uniform. Form dough into 6 patties, each about 2 inches wide. TIP: You can use a food processor for this step if you'd prefer.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil to a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add black bean patties and cook until crisp and heated through, 3 to 5 minutes per side. TIP: Add more oil as necessary to crisp the patties.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add baby spinach, remaining lime juice, 2 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a large bowl, and toss. Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and thinly slice the flesh. Divide spinach salad between bowls and top with black bean patties, diced cucumber, halved tomatoes, and sliced avocado. Drizzle with lime aioli. Bon appétit! Aioli vs Mayo—What's the Difference? Both have a creamy consistency and whitish color. But mayonnaise is an emulsion made with egg yolk and oil, and aioli is an emulsion created with just garlic and extra virgin olive oil. Sometimes egg yolk is added as well, but even then, the addition of garlic sets aioli apart from mayo. In this recipe we put our own spin on things by making lime aioli, which includes minced garlic, lime zest, lime juice, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt. It's so good!

recipes.title: Potato Malai Kofta
recipes.subtitle: with Sautéed Spinach & Tomato Chutney
recipes.summary: with Sautéed Spinach & Tomato Chutney

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the potato into bite-size peces. Add diced potato to a medium saucepan and cover with 1 inch salted water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes. TIP: Don't drain the potatoes. They will continue to cook in step 2.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and chop the onion. Peel and slice the garlic. Peel and mince just 2 tsp ginger. Slice green beans into rounds. Peel and grate carrot on the largest side of a box grater. When boiled potatoes are fork-tender, add sliced green beans and grated carrot to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute. Drain cooked vegetables and transfer to a large bowl. TIPS: We will use this saucepan again in the next step. Save the remining ginger for your own use.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Return the empty saucepan to medium heat with 2 tsp olive oil. Add chopped onion, sliced garlic, and minced ginger, and cook until softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add tomato power, curry powder, coconut milk, and ½ tsp salt, and bring to a simmer. Cook the malai sauce until slightly hickened, 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add garbanzo bean flour and ¼ tsp salt to the large bowl with the cooked vegetables. Lightly mash kofta mixture and form into 6 patties, each about 1 inch thick.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add kofta patties and cook until browned and crisp, 1 to 2 minutes per side. Transfer the crispy potato kofta to a plate.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium heat with 1 tsp olive oil. Add baby spinach and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until wilted, 2 to 3 minutes. Transfer malai sauce to a blender and blend on low until smooth. Divide malai sauce between plates and add sautéed spinach and crispy potato kofta. Top the potato malai kofta with tomato chutney. Bon appétit! TIP: Vent blender (by removing the plastic center of the lid) and begin blending on low and finish on high to avoid splashing.

recipes.title: Curried Carrot Fritters
recipes.subtitle: with Mango Avocado Salad & Tamarind Chutney
recipes.summary: with Mango Avocado Salad & Tamarind Chutney

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add garbanzo bean flour, curry powder, and ½ cup cold water to a large bowl and whisk to combine. Grate carrots using the large side of a box grater. Add 2 cups grated carrot, ½ tsp salt and ¼ tsp pepper to the large bowl with the batter. Mix the carrot batter and set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and mince the shallot. Peel and mince the garlic. Peel the mango, remove the pit, and thinly slice the flesh. Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and thinly slice the flesh. Halve the grape tomatoes. Pick and roughly chop the parsley leaves. Zest, halve, and juice the lime.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add minced shallot, minced garlic, sliced mango, sliced avocado, halved tomatoes, chopped parsley leaves, lime zest, lime juice, ½ tsp salt, and a pinch of pepper to a large bowl. Toss the mango avocado salad and refrigerate until ready to serve.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Scoop 6 portions of carrot batter into the skillet, each about ¼ cup. Use a measuring cup bottom to flatten the fritters, and cook fritters until browned and crisp, 3 to 5 minutes per side. Transfer curried carrot fritters to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: If necessary, work in batches to avoid overcrowding the skillet.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Divide the curried carrot fritters between plates and drizzle with tamarind chutney. Serve with mango avocado salad. Dig in! Madras-Style Curry Powder: Pure Delight Madras curry originated in southeastern India and is well known for its warm, aromatic quality. Our spice blend recreates that essence with a sophisticated mix of turmeric, cumin, coriander, and fenugreek, adding tons of flavor to your meal.

recipes.title: Italian Sausage Sandwiches
recipes.subtitle: with Shallot Relish & Dilly Cucumber Salad
recipes.summary: with Shallot Relish & Dilly Cucumber Salad
ingredients: 1 oz Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Thinly slice the cucumber into rounds. Thinly slice just 4 garlic cloves. Add sliced cucumber, sliced garlic, white vinegar, dill, sugar, and a pinch of salt to a large bowl, and toss the dilly cucumber salad to combine. TIP: Continue to toss the cucumbers occasionally to ensure they are evenly coated.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Set the oven to broil. Peel and quarter the shallots. Add quartered shallots, 2 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt to a baking sheet, and toss. Broil until softened and well-browned in places, 7 to 10 minutes. Let broiled shallots cool for 5 minutes, transfer to a cutting board, and roughly chop into bite-size pieces. Thinly slice the Thai chile. In a medium bowl, combine chopped shallots, as much sliced chile as you'd like, balsamic glaze, and agave. Toss the shallot relish and sprinkle with salt. TIP: All broilers are different. Watch carefully to ensure the shallots don't burn.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Peel and mince the remaining garlic clove and add to a small bowl. Add Vegenaise and a pinch of salt. Stir the garlic aioli to combine.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Remove sausages from the plastic casing and slice. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced sausage and cook until browned on both sides, 4 to 6 minutes. Slice ciabatta rolls in half and drizzle cut side of halved rolls with 1 tsp olive oil. Sprinkle with a pinch of salt and broil in the oven, directly on the rack, until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Spread the bottom halves of the toasted ciabatta rolls with garlic aioli. Top with cooked sausage, shallot relish, and baby arugula. Serve Italian sausage sandwiches with dilly cucumber salad. Enjoy! сл Want Some Fresh Air While Cooking? Then we suggest you fire up the grill! You can cook these tasty Italian sausages in your kitchen, of course, but they also cook up nicely on the grill-gas or charcoal. So if you've got a grill, now's the perfect time to put it to use.

recipes.title: Beluga Lentil Tacos
recipes.subtitle: with Quick Guacamole & Radish Jalapeño Slaw
recipes.summary: with Quick Guacamole & Radish Jalapeño Slaw
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat oven to 350°F. Rinse and sort the beluga lentils. Add beluga lentils, cumin, vegetable broth concentrate, and 1¼ cups water to a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender and water is absorbed, 20 to 25 minutes. TIPS: The oven will be used to warm the tortillas. You can use a toster oven or microwave instead if you prefer. To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally ocurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Trim and thinly slice the red radishes. Thinly slice the jalapeño. TIP: If you're sensitive to spice, deseed the jalapeño before slicing.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Halve and juice 1 lime and cut the remaining lime into wedges. Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and scoop the flesh into a medium bowl. Add just half the lime juice and a pinch of salt and pepper, and mash the quick guacamole with a fork.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add sliced red radish, just a few jalapeño slices, remaining lime juice, red cabbage, 1 tsp olive oil, and ⅛ tsp salt to a medium bowl. Toss the radish jalapeño slaw.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add Vegenaise, chipotle morita powder, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl. Mix the chipotle crema. TIP: If you're sensitive to spice, omit or use less chipotle morita powder.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Wrap corn tortillas in foil and warm in the oven for 4 to 5 minutes. Top warm corn tortillas with quick guacamole, cooked beluga lentils, and radish jalapeño slaw. Dollop the beluga lentil tacos with chipotle crema and serve with lime wedges and any remaining radish jalapeño slaw. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Mushroom Cheddar Black Bean Burgers
recipes.subtitle: with Garlic Aioli & Carrot Fries
recipes.summary: with Garlic Aioli & Carrot Fries
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Peel the carrots, slice into sticks, and place on a baking sheet. Add 2 tsp (4 tsp) vegetable oil, toss to coat, and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Bake carrot fries until tender and browned in spots, 18 to 22 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Thinly slice the mushrooms. Halve the onion(s), mince one half (1 onion), and thinly slice the other half (1 onion). Heat 2 tsp (4 tsp) vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced mushrooms, sliced onion, and just ½ tsp (1 tsp) fajita seasoning Cook until mushrooms are browned in places, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer the sautéed vegetables to a bowl. running water, and wipe mushrooms clean with a damp kitchen towel. We will use this skillet again.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Drain and rinse the black beans. Peel and mince the garlic. Add just ½ cup (1 cup) minced onion, remaining fajita seasoning, black beans, just half the minced garlic, and flour to a large bowl. Mix with your hands, crushing the beans, until burger mix is sticky and uniform, and form into 2 (4) patties about 1 inch thick. TIP: Keep any remaining minced onion for your on use.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) vegetable oil in the large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add black bean patties and cook until crisp and heated through, 3 to 5 minutes per side. Remove skillet from heat, top cooked black bean patties with cheddar, and cover until melted, 3 to 5 minutes. TIP: Add more oil as necessary to crisp the burgers.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add remaining minced garlic, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl. Mix the garlic aioli.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Toast the potato buns. Spread garlic aioli on the top half of each bun, and place black bean patties on the bottom halves. Top with sautéed vegetables. Serve the mushroom cheddar black bean burgers with carrot fries and garlic aoli for dipping. Dig in!

recipes.subtitle: with Dijon Mustard & Radishes

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Drain and rinse the chickpeas. Halve and juice the lemon. Trim and thinly slice the radishes. Add chickpeas to a large bowl and mash with a fork. Add lemon juice, Vegenaise, Dijon mustard, sunflower seeds, and a pinch of salt and pepper to the mashed chickpeas, and stir.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Toast the sourdough bread. Add half the sunflower chickpea salad and some sliced radish to one slice of sourdough toast. Top with another slice of sourdough toast to close the sandwich. Repeat to make the second sandwich.

recipes.subtitle: with Jalapeño & Sesame Butter
recipes.summary: with Jalapeño & Sesame Butter

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Trim, deseed, and thinly slice the jalapeño. Roughly chop the kimchi. Spread butter on each slice of sourdough bread, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add 2 slices of sourdough bread, butter side down. Sprinkle ¼ of the cheddar cheese on each slice, and add sliced jalapeño and chopped kimchi. Top with remaining cheddar cheese and close sandwiches with the remaining slices of bread, with the butter side up. Continue to cook sandwiches until they're browned on both sides, 3 to 4 minutes per side. Cut sandwiches in half and serve.

recipes.title: Green Curry Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Basil & Thai Tomato Salad
recipes.summary: with Basil & Thai Tomato Salad

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the noodles. Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Peel and mince the ginger. Peel and slice the shallot. Pick the basil leaves. Drain tofu, pat dry, and slice into 1 inch pieces.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add green beans and a pinch of salt and cook until beans are charred in places, 3-4 minutes. Transfer charred green beans to a medium bowl. Halve the lime, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Add lime juice to the bowl with the green beans. Roughly chop the peanuts. Halve the grape tomatoes. Add chopped peanuts, halved tomatoes, and tamari to the green beans and toss the Thai tomato salad. TIP: We will use this skillet again in a later step.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Add sliced garlic, minced ginger, and sliced shallot, and cook until fragrant, 2-3 minutes. Add green curry paste, coconut milk, turbinado sugar, salt and ¼ cup water. Bring green curry sauce to a simmer, add basil leaves, and turn off the heat.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Once the water in the large pot is boiling, add rice noodles, stir, and remove from heat. Stir occasionally until noodles are tender, 12 minutes. Drain noodles and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process, and return noodles to the pot.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in the large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced tofu and a pinch of salt. Cook, tossing occasionally, until tofu is crispy in places, 5 to 6 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Pour green curry sauce over the rice noodles and stir until noodles are coated. Divide the green curry noodles and tofu between large bowls. Top with Thai tomato salad and garnish with lime wedges. Dig in!

recipes.title: Tahini Cauliflower Steaks
recipes.subtitle: with Red Lentil Skordalia & Charred Green Beans
recipes.summary: with Red Lentil Skordalia & Charred Green Beans

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Peel potato and dice into 1 inch pieces. Pick and roughly chop dill fronds. Remove cauliflower leaves and cut 1 inch off the stem. Carefully cut cauliflower into 4 "steaks," each about 1 inch thick. Peel and mince the garlic. Halve the lemon, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. TIP: Roast any leftover cauliflower florets along with the steaks.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add diced potato, red lentils, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are soft and potatoes are forktender, 14 to 16 minutes. Mash the red lentil skordalia with a fork.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Spread 2 tsp oil on a baking sheet. In a medium bowl, combine just half the minced garlic, just 1 tbsp lemon juice, tahini, smoked paprika, 2 tbsp olive oil, and ¾ tsp salt. Whisk until mixture and place on the baking sheet. Roast until tender and well browned, 15 to 20 minutes. TIP: If you prefer, you can use foil or parchment paper to line the baking sheet instead of adding oil.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add just half the chopped dill, remaining minced garlic, white vinegar, and 1 tbsp olive oil to the saucepan with the red lentil skordalia. Mash with a fork until mostly smooth. Taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add green beans and cook until charred in places, 5 to 8 minutes. Taste, add a pinch of salt and pepper, and remove from heat.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the red lentil skordalia between two sprinkle with pumpkin seeds and remaining chopped dill. Serve with charred green beans and lemon wedges. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Fettuccine Arrabbiata
recipes.subtitle: with Roasted Broccoli & Basil Pesto
recipes.summary: with Roasted Broccoli & Basil Pesto

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Peel and dice the onion(s). Peel and mince the garlic. Chop broccoli florets into bite-size peces.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add fettuccine to the boiling water and cook until al dente, 10 to 12 minutes. Drain pasta and return to the pot, off heat.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add chopped broccoli florets, 1 tsp (2 tsp) olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a baking sheet, and toss. Roast until browned and crisp in places, 10 to 12 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add panko breadcrumbs, 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil, and ¼ tsp (½ tsp) salt, and stir frequently until breadcrumbs are golden brown, 2 to 4 minutes. Transfer toasted panko breadcrumbs to a small bowl. TIP: We will use this saucepan again in the next step.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return the medium saucepan to medium-high heat with 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil. Once hot, add diced onion, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until onion is softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add tomatoes, Italian seasoning blend, ¼ tsp (½ tsp) red chile flakes, and ½ tsp (1 tsp) salt. Cook the arrabbiata sauce until it begins to simmer, 3 to 5 minutes. TIP: If you're sensitive to spice, add less of the red chile flakes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Add arrabbiata sauce to the pot with the fettuccine, and stir. Divide the fettuccine arrabbiata between large bowls, ladling any remaining arrabbiata sauce over the pasta. Serve with roasted broccoli and top with toasted panko breadcrumbs, basil pesto, and parmesan. Enjoy!

ingredients: ¼ cup Forager Project® Organic Dairy-Free Cashewmilk Yogurt

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Thinly slice cucumber(s) into rounds. Peel and thinly slice 1 cup (2 cups) onion. Thinly slice the seitan. Halve and juice the lemon(s). Thinly slice the tomato(es). Pick the mint leaves. TIP: The oven will be used to toast the flatbreads. If you have a toaster oven you can use that instead.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add sliced cucumber, Champagne vinegar, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a medium bowl, and toss to coat. Set quick pickles aside until step 5.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced seitan and a pinch of salt and cook until seitan is browned in places, 4 to 6 minutes. Sprinkle crispy seitan with just 1 tsp (2 tsp) ras el hanout and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. TIP: Add more ras el hanout if you prefer.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add yogurt, just 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) lemon juice, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl, and whisk the lemon yogurt. Place flatbreads directly on the oven rack and toast until warm, 2 to 3 minutes. TIP: Don't let the flatbreads get too crispy. They should still be pliable enough to fold.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Top toasted flatbreads with crispy seitan, quick pickles, as much sliced onion as you'd like, and sliced tomato. Top the Greek seitan gyros with mint leaves and lemon yogurt. To eat, fold one side of the flatbread over the other, like a taco. Dig in! Crispy Seitan = Amazing Seitan We love seitan because it's high in protein, mimics the texture of meat, and impressively soaks up flavor. And while there are many ways to serve it, in this recipe it's all about creating some crisp. To make delightfully crispy seitan, get the pan nice and hot, somewhere between medium-high CH and high heat-and make sure you're using enough oil. Finally, toss the seitan occasionally, not too often!

recipes.title: Roasted Red Pepper Shakshuka
recipes.subtitle: with Za'atar Tofu & Crispy Shallots
recipes.summary: with Za'atar Tofu & Crispy Shallots
ingredients: 2 oz Treeline® Soft French-Style Cashew Cheese

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Crumble the tofu into bite-size peces. Peel and mince the shallot(s) and garlic. Drain the red peppers. Pick the mint leaves from the stems.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Place the crumbled tofu on a baking sheet and toss with 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil, za'atar seasoning, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Bake until browned in places, 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 3 tbsp (6 tbsp) olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the minced shallot, minced garlic, and cumin seeds. Cook until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. Add the red peppers, tomatoes, ½ tsp (1 tsp) salt, and a pinch of pepper. Bring to asimmer and cook until the sauce hickens, 5 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Place the multigrain flatbread(s) directly on the oven rack. Bake until slightly crisp, 3 to 5 minutes. Add the baked za'atar tofu to the sauce and simmer 2 to 3 minutes. Remove the roasted red pepper shakshuka from heat and dollop with cashew cheese. Top with crispy shallots and mint leaves. Serve family style with crispy flatbread on the side. Enjoy! What is shakshuka? Though it originated in Northern Africa, Shakshuka has been embraced by cultures around the world, particularly in Israel where it's one of the country's national dishes. Traditionally, eggs are poached in a smoky, spicy, vegetable-packed tomato sauce, but in this recipe we replace eggs with cashew cheese. The result is a tasty protein source. Ultimately, no matter which ingredients you include, this one-skillet wonder is a delicious meal that can be served for breakfast, lunch, or dinner!

recipes.title: Parmesan Gnocchi
recipes.subtitle: with Melted Summer Squash & Basil
recipes.summary: with Melted Summer Squash & Basil

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Trim the yellow squash and slice into half moons. Halve the lemon(s), juice one half (1 lemon), and divide the other half (1 lemon) into wedges. Pick the basil leaves from the stems.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the sliced garlic and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until garlic is lightly browned, 2 to 3 minutes. Transfer the crispy garlic to a small bowl and return skillet to medium-high heat.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the sliced yellow squash to the skillet and cook until lightly browned in places, 3 to 4 minutes. Reduce heat to low and cook until the squash softens and breaks down, another 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer the melted summer squash to a bowl. TIP: We will reuse this skillet in a later step.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the gnocchi to the large pot of boiling water and cook until the gnocchi floats, 3 to 5 minutes. Drain the cooked gnocchi and shake to remove excess water.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return skillet to medium-high heat with the butter. Once melted, add the cooked gnocchi and cook, undisturbed, until browned in places, 2 to 4 minutes. Add the parmesan and melted summer squash and stir to combine. Remove skillet from heat and add the lemon juice.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the parmesan gnocchi with melted summer squash between bowls. Top with basil leaves, crispy garlic, and as much of the Aleppo pepper as you'd like. Serve with lemon wedges. Dig in!

recipes.subtitle: with Crispy Shiitakes & Quick Pickles
recipes.summary: with Crispy Shiitakes & Quick Pickles

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the soba noodles. Peel and thinly slice the cucumber. Add sliced cucumber, just 2 tbsp rice vinegar, just ½ tsp agave, and ⅛ tsp salt to a medium bowl. Toss to combine, and set the quick pickles aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Destem Lacinato kale and thinly slice the leaves. Trim shiitake mushroom stems and thinly slice the caps. Peel and mince the garlic. Add remaining rice vinegar, remaining agave, minced garlic, tahini, tamari, sesame oil, 2 tbsp hot water, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl and whisk the tahini sauce. water, and wipe mushrooms clean with a damp kitchen towel.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced shiitake mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until well browned, 5 to 6 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: We will reuse this skillet in a later step.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Once the water is boiling, add soba noodles and gently stir. Cook until al dente, 2 to 3 minutes. Drain noodles and run under cool water to stop the cooking process.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return skillet to medium-high heat and add sliced Lacinato kale and 1 tbsp water. Cook until kale is wilted, 2 to 3 minutes. Remove skillet from heat, add cooked soba noodles and tahini sauce to the kale, and stir.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the creamy tahini noodles between large bowls and top with crispy shiitakes and quick pickles. Enjoy.

recipes.title: Kashmiri Chickpea Bowls
recipes.subtitle: with Yogurt Smashed Vegetables & Golden Raisins
recipes.summary: with Yogurt Smashed Vegetables & Golden Raisins
ingredients: ¼ cup Forager Project® Organic Dairy-Free Cashewmilk Yogurt

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Add jasmine rice, 1 cup water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until water is absorbed and grains are tender, 14 to 16 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Destem curly kale and roughly chop the leaves. Place cucumber and radishes on a cutting board. Smash egetables by using the flat side of a knife and applying pressure with the heel of your hand until the egetables split. Tear the smashed egetables into bite-size peces. Peel and mince the garlic. Halve and juice the lemon. Drain and rinse the chickpeas, and pat dry.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Place chickpeas on a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, ½ tsp Kasmiri chile powder, and a pinch of salt. Roast until crispy, 12 to 15 minutes. TIPS: Cover the chickpeas with a wire rack if they begin to pop in the oven. The Kasmiri chile powder is spicy; use more or less if you prefer.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add minced garlic, just 1 tbsp lemon juice, yogurt, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a medium bowl, and stir the yogurt dessing. Add smashed egetables and toss to coat.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add chopped curly kale and a pinch of salt and pepper, and cook until the greens are wilted, 1 to 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Fluff the cooked jasmine rice with a fork and add remaining lemon juice, dill, golden raisins, and 1 tsp olive oil. Gently stir the pilaf to combine. Divide pilaf and cooked curly kale between shallow bowls. Top with kashmiri chickpeas and yogurt smashed vegetables. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Sicilian-Style Broccoli
recipes.subtitle: with Tarragon Hazelnut Relish & Lemon Caper Millet
recipes.summary: with Tarragon Hazelnut Relish & Lemon Caper Millet

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and mince the shallot. Heat 1 tsp olive oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add minced shallot and cook until softened, 1 to 2 minutes. Add millet and toast until aromatic and beginning to pop, 3 to 5 minutes. Add 1 cup water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until the water is absorbed and grains are tender, 17 to 20 minutes. Remove cooked millet from heat and set aside, covered.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Cut the broccoli crown into large florets and thinly slice. Trim and roughly chop the broccoli rabe. Pick and roughly chop the tarragon leaves. Peel and mince the garlic. Halve and juice the lemon.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium heat and add hazelnuts. Cook, shaking the pan occasionally, until nuts are toasted, 3 to 5 minutes. Let toasted hazelnuts cool, and transfer to a cutting board. Remove skins and roughly chop the nuts. Add chopped tarragon, minced garlic, chopped hazelnuts, 2 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl. Stir the tarragon hazelnut relish.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium-heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add liced broccoli florets and cook until until crisp-tender, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Add chopped broccoli rabe and cook until bright green and just wilted, 3 to 5 minutes. Sprinkle charred broccoli with salt and add just half the lemon juice.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Fluff the cooked millet with a fork. Add remaining lemon juice, capers, golden raisins, and a pinch of salt, and stir. Divide the lemon caper millet between plates. Top with Sicilian-style broccoli and tarragon hazelnut relish. Tuck in! Culinary Inspo? Caponata! When creating this dish our culinary team was inspired by caponata—described by Martha Rose Shulman, a food columnist for The New York Times, as "a Sicilian sweet and sour version of ratatouille." It's sweet, savory, and a little nutty, just like this dish. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Kashmiri Roasted Vegetables & Tofu Paneer
recipes.subtitle: with Cucumber Raita & Millet
recipes.summary: with Cucumber Raita & Millet

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Add millet to a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Toast until millet is aromatic and begins to pop, 3 to 5 minutes. Add 1 cup (2 cups) water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until the water is absorbed and grains are tender, 17 to 20 minutes. Remove cooked millet from heat and set aside, covered.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Drain tofu and cut into cubes. Peel and roughly chop carrots. Peel and mince the garlic and ginger. Add minced garlic, minced ginger, 2 tsp Kashmiri chile power, tomato powder, 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) vegetable oil, 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) warm water, and ½ tsp (1 tsp) salt to a medium bowl and whisk the Kasmiri marinade. TIP: Add more Kashmiri chile power if you prefer.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Place cubed tofu, chopped carrot, and grape tomatoes on a baking sheet, add Kasmiri marinade, and toss to coat. Roast until tofu is browned in places and carrots are tender, 23 to 25 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Grate cucumber(s) on the largest side of a box grater. Add grated cucumber to a medium bowl. Halve and juice the lemon(s). Add just half the lemon juice, sour cream, and a pinch of salt to the cucumber. Stir the cucumber raita to combine.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Drizzle remaining lemon juice over roasted tofu and vegetables. Divide cooked millet between bowls. Top with Kashmiri roasted vegetables & tofu paneer. Serve with cucumber raita and mango chutney. Tuck in! Kashmiri Chile The term "Kashmiri chile" is actually somewhat of a misnomer. Due to the extremely small quantity of chiles grown in the Kashmir region of India, most are typically reserved for local consumption. So most Kashmiri chile powder on the market isn't made with authentic Kashmiri chiles. Ours is made using finely ground red chiles from India and it has a rich red color and moderate heat level.

recipes.title: Pan Seared Broccolini
recipes.subtitle: with Mashed Sweet Potatoes & Coconut Turmeric Relish
recipes.summary: with Mashed Sweet Potatoes & Coconut Turmeric Relish

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the sweet potato(es). Add diced sweet potato to a small saucepan and cover with 1 inch water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes. Drain and return cooked sweet potato to the saucepan, off heat. Rinse and sort the beluga lentils, and add to a separate saucepan with 1 cup (2 cups) water. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender, 20 to 24 minutes. TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally ocurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Trim, halve, and deseed the Fresno chile(s), and mince one half (1 Fresno chile). Peel and mince 2 tsp (4 tsp) ginger. Halve and juice the lemon(s). Trim 1 inch off the broccolini stems. TIP: Save the remining ginger for your own use.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add sliced garlic and cook until lightly browned, 3 to 5 minutes. Add minced Fresno chile, minced ginger, golden raisins, coconut, brown mustard seeds, and turmeric. Stir and cook until aromatic, 1 to 3 minutes. Add lemon juice and transfer the coconut turmeric relish to a medium bowl. TIP: Add more Fresno chile if you prefer more heat. Save any remaining chile for your own use.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Wipe the skillet clean and return to medium-high heat. Add 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil, ¼ cup (½ cup) water, and a pinch of salt. Add broccolini, cover, and cook until water evaporates and broccolini is tender and crispy in places, 3 to 5 minutes. Flip and cook for an additional 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add butter to the cooked sweet potatoes and mash with a fork. Taste, and add salt as necessary. Divide the mashed sweet potatoes and cooked beluga lentils between large plates. Top with pan seared broccolini and coconut turmeric relish. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Coconut Tofu
recipes.subtitle: with Crunchy Date Slaw & Peanut Sauce
recipes.summary: with Crunchy Date Slaw & Peanut Sauce

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add sushi rice, 1 cup water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until the grains are tender and water is absorbed, 13 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Pat tofu dry and cut into 8 slices. Finely chop the dates, removing the pits. Halve and juice the lime. Add chopped dates, lime juice, red cabbage, just 1 tsp sesame seeds, 1 tsp vegetable oil, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl. Toss the crunchy date slaw.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Chop the toasted coconut. Add remaining sesame seeds, chopped coconut, and breadcrumbs to a plate, and mix. In a separate bowl, combine cornstarch and ¼ cup cold water and whisk. TIP: Instead of chopping the toasted coconut, you can also seal it in a plastic bag and crush with a rolling pin or mug

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Toss sliced tofu in the cornstarch mixture, remove, and press into the coconut breadcrumbs. Heat ¼ cup vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add breaded tofu and cook until browned, 3 to 4 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate, sprinkle with salt, and cover loosely to keep warm.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Wipe the skillet clean and return to medium-low heat. Add spicy peanut sauce and 2 tbsp water. Cook until hot, 1 to 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the rice and crunchy date slaw between plates. Top with coconut tofu and serve with peanut sauce for dipping. Bon appétit!

ingredients: 2 oz Treeline® Soft French-Style Cashew Cheese

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Rinse and sort the beluga lentils. Add lentils and 1¼ cups (2½ cups) water to a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender and water is absorbed, 20 to 23 minutes. Drain any remaining water, transfer lentils to a bowl, and rinse the saucepan. TIP: We will use this saucepan again

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Halve sweet potatoes lengthwise, and cut each half into 3 to 4 long wedges. Transfer to a baking sheet cut side down, coat with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil, and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Roast until tender, 22 to 26 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Peel the oranges and chop into bite-size pieces. Pick cilantro leaves and reserve the tender stems. Add chopped orange, cilantro leaves and stems, pecans, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl, and stir to combine. Crumble cashew cheese into the bowl, and gently toss the citrus pecan salad. Peel and mince the garlic.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: In a small bowl, combine the yogurt and ⅛ tsp (¼ tsp) chipotle morita spice. Stir the chipotle crema. TIP: Add more chipotle morita spice if you prefer.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return the medium saucepan to medium heat with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil. Add minced garlic and cook until golden and toasted 4 2 to 3 minutes. Add cooked lentils and cook until heated through. Taste, and add salt as necessary.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Slightly mash the sweet potatoes with a fork. Smear chipotle crema on large plates. Top with roasted sweet potatoes, beluga lentils and citrus pecan salad. Drizzle with olive oil. Bon appétit!

steps.text: Add farro and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and cover with 1 inch cold water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and cook until farro is tender, 18 to 20 minutes. Drain farro and return to the saucepan, off heat.

steps.title: Step 6

recipes.subtitle: with Red Lentil Skordalia & Charred Green Beans
recipes.summary: with Red Lentil Skordalia & Charred Green Beans

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Peel russet potato and dice into 1 inch peces. Pick and roughly chop dill fronds. Remove califlower leaves and cut 1 inch off the stem. Carefully cut cauliflower into 4 "steaks," each about 1 inch thick. Peel and mince the garlic. Halve the lemon, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. TIP: Roast any remaining califlower florets along with the steaks.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add diced russet potato, red lentils, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are soft and potatoes are fork-tender, 14 to 16 minutes. Mash the red lentil skordalia with a fork.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Line a baking sheet with foil or parchment paper. In a medium bowl, combine just half the minced garlic, just 1 tbsp lemon juice, tahini, smoked paprika, 2 tbsp olive oil, and ¾ tsp salt. Whisk until combined. Coat cauliflower steaks with tahini miture and place on the lined baking sheet. Roast until tender and well browned, 15 to 20 minutes. TIP: If you don't have foil or parchment paper, add 2 tsp oil to the baking sheet instead (though the cauliflower may stick slightly).

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add just half the chopped dill, remaining minced garlic, white vinegar, and 1 tbsp olive oil to the saucepan with the red lentil skordalia. Mash with a fork until mostly smooth. Taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add green beans and cook until they are charred in places, 5 to 8 minutes. Taste, add salt and pepper as necessary, and remove from heat.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide red lentil skordalia between two large sprinkle with pumpkin seeds and remaining chopped dill. Serve with charred green beans and lemon wedges. Dig in!

recipes.ocr_text:

recipes.title: Falafel Spiced Tofu
recipes.subtitle: with Pickled Cauliflower & Creamy Cashew Dressing
recipes.summary: with Pickled Cauliflower & Creamy Cashew Dressing

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Add cashews and ½ cup hot water to a small bowl. Crumble tofu into bite-size pieces and add to a large bowl. Peel and mince the garlic. Add just half the minced garlic to the bowl with the tofu, along with zhoug seasoning, 2 tsp egetable oil, Y½ tsp salt, and a pinch of pepper. Toss to coat.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Line a baking sheet with foil or parchment paper. Place tofu miture on the baking sheet and bake until browned and crispy in places, 25 to 30 minutes. TIP: If you don't have foil or parchment paper, add 2 tsp oil to the baking sheet instead.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Halve and juice the lemon. Destem and thinly slice the curly kale. Check the Castelvetrano olives, remove any pits, and roughly chop the olives. Drain the roasted red peppers.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the cashews, their soaking water, remaining minced garlic, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a bender. Blend until smooth. Taste the creamy cashew dressing and add salt as necessary.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the sliced curly kale and 2 tsp olive oil to a large bowl, and massage kale until bright green. Add chopped Castelvetrano olives, roasted red peppers, and just half the creamy cashew dressing, and toss to combine.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the kale salad between bowls. Top with falafel spiced tofu and pickled cauliflower. Drizzle with remaining creamy cashew dressing. Bon appétit!

recipes.title: Sausage Jambalaya
recipes.subtitle: with Kale & Long Grain Rice
recipes.summary: with Kale & Long Grain Rice

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the onion. Trim, deseed, and dice the green bell pepper. Dice the celery. Peel and mince the garlic. Desteem the curly kale and roughly chop the leaves.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Remove sausages from the plastic casing and slice into rounds. Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add liced sausage and cook, stirring occasionally, until it begins to crisp, 4 to 5 minutes. Add chopped curly kale and cook until kale is bright green and wilted, 1 to 2 minutes. Transfer sautéed sausage and curly kale to a plate.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Return the pot to medium-high heat. Add diced onion, diced green bell pepper, and diced celery to the pot and cook until softened, 3 to 4 minutes. Add minced garlic and a pinch of salt and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the basmati rice, chipotle morita powder, tomatoes, vegetable broth concentrate, and 1¼ cups water to the pot. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until the water is absorbed and rice is tender, 15 to 18 minutes. TIP: If you're sensitive to spice, omit or use less chipotle.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the sautéed sausage and curly kale to the jambalaya and cook until warm, 1 to 2 minutes. Divide the sausage jambalaya with kale and long-grain rice between large bowls. Dig in! A Divine and Delicious Base You've probably heard of mirepoix, the French term for a flavor base made from sautéed onion, carrot, and celery. It's a key component of many of the soups, stews, and sauces you love. But say you swap in green peppers for carrots, what's that called? In the southeastern United States, people call that mixture the Holy Trinity, and it's the fondation of classic Cajun dishes like gumbo and jambalaya.

recipes.title: Cavatappi Bolognese
recipes.subtitle: with Rosemary & Parmesan
recipes.summary: with Rosemary & Parmesan

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the pasta. Peel and roughly chop the shallot and garlic. Peel and roughly chop the carrot. Pick the rosemary leaves, and mince. Trim the broccolini and chop into 2 inch pieces.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the chopped shallot and chopped garlic to a food processor, and pulse until finely chopped Add chopped carrot and cauliflower florets to the shallot and garlic in the food processor and pulse until pieces are the size of rice grains, 10 to 12 pulses.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add cavatappi to the boiling water, stir, and cook until al dente, 8 to 10 minutes. Reserve ¾ cup pasta water, drain the pasta, and return pasta to the large pot, off heat. Toss cooked cavatappi with 1 tsp olive oil to prevent sticking.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add processed vegetable mixture and cook, stirring occasionally, until shallot is softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add red chile flakes and ½ tsp minced rosemary and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the crushed tomatoes, red miso paste, and reserved pasta water to the skillet and stir to dissolve the miso. Cook, stirring occasionally, until cauliflower is tender and sauce has thickened, 4 to 6 minutes. Add ½ tsp salt, taste, and add more salt as necessary.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the cooked cavatappi between large plates and top with bolognese sauce. Return the large pot to medium-high heat with 1 tsp olive oil. Add chopped broccolini, cook until crisp-tender, and sprinkle with salt. Add cooked broccolini to the plates and top everything with parmesan. Mangia!
