recipes.title: Crispy Carrot Dumplings
recipes.subtitle: with Hazelnut Sesame Butter & Apple Arugula Salad
recipes.summary: with Hazelnut Sesame Butter & Apple Arugula Salad

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a medium saucepan of salted water to a boil for the carrots. Peel carrots and thinly slice into rounds. Finely chop the hazelnuts. Finely chop the parsley leaves. Thinly slice the apple. Add liced apple, Champagne vinegar, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl and toss.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Once the water is boiling, add sliced carrots and cook until fork-tender, 6 to 7 minutes. Drain boiled carrots and transfer to a food processor. Add just half the chopped parsley. cream cheese, pumpkin pie spice, and ½ tsp salt to the food processor and blend until smooth, scraping down the sides as necessary.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Place dumpling wrappers on a flat surface and fill a small bowl with cold water. Use a tablespoon to place carrot filling in the center of each wrapper. Dip your finger in the cold water and wet the rim of each wrapper. Fold dumpling wrappers over the filling and press down to seal, pinching tightly.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Melt butter in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add chopped hazelnuts, white sesame seeds, and a pinch of salt. Cook, tossing frequently, until fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes Use a slotted spoon to transfer toasted nuts and sesame seeds to a bowl, reserving the butter in the killet. TIP: The nuts and seeds can burn quickly, so reduce heat if necessary.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Increase the heat to medium-high. Add half the carrot dumplings to the skillet and cook until browned and crispy, 2 to 3 minutes. Flip dumplings and cook for 1 minute. Add ¼ cup water, reduce heat to medium, and cover. Steam crispy carrot dumplings until tender and the water has evaporated, 3 to 4 minutes. Repeat with remaining dumplings.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Add baby arugula, 1 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of pepper to the bowl with the apples. Toss the apple arugula salad to coat evenly. Divide the crispy carrot dumplings between plates and sprinkle with toasted hazelnuts and sesame seeds and remaining chopped parsley. Serve the apple arugula salad on the side. Dig in!

recipes.title: Japanese Yam Sushi Bowls
recipes.subtitle: with Sesame Spinach & Citrus Aioli
recipes.summary: with Sesame Spinach & Citrus Aioli
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Cut Japanese yams into wedges and place on a baking sheet. Toss with 2 tsp egetable oil and a pinch of salt and pepper, and bake until wedges are crisp and slightly browned, 20 to 25 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the quinoa speckled rice, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until the water is absorbed and rice is tender, 15 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Thinly slice the cucumber. Add liced cucumber, chile garlic sauce, and rice vinegar to a medium bowl and toss.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Zest and halve the lime, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Add lime zest, lime juice, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl. Mix the citrus aioli.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add baby spinach, just 2 tsp tuxedo sesame seeds, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until spinach is wilted, 1 to 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the cooked quinoa speckled rice between large bowls. Add roasted Japanese yam wedges, sesame spinach, and spicy cucumbers, and drizzle with citrus aioli. Tear the nori sheet into pieces and sprinkle over the bowls. Top the Japanese yam sushi bowls with remaining tuxedo sesame seeds. Serve with lime wedges. Itadakimasu! (In Japan, this is often said before a meal to express thanks to whomever prepared the food.)

recipes.title: Calabrian Bean & Veggie Bowls
recipes.subtitle: with Toasted Farro & Basil Cashew Sauce
recipes.summary: with Toasted Farro & Basil Cashew Sauce
ingredients: 5.3 oz Brami® Hot Chili Pepper Italian Snacking Lupini Beans

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Add farro and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and cover with 1 inch cold water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and cook until farro is tender, 18 to 20 minutes. Drain well and return farro to the saucepan, uncovered, off heat.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add cashews to a small bowl and cover with ⅓ cup hot water. Peel garlic cloves. Trim one inch off the broccolini stems. Peel parsnip and chop into bite-size peces. Drain and finely chop the roasted red peppers. Halve and juice the lime.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add broccolini to one side of a baking sheet and chopped parsnip to the other side, and toss vegetables with 1 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until tender and browned in places, 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Pick the basil leaves. Add cashews and their soaking water, just 1 peeled garlic clove, lime juice, just half the basil leaves, just 1 tbsp white sesame seeds, and a pinch of salt to a blender. Blend the basil cashew sauce until smooth.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Thinly slice remaining peeled garlic cloves. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add cooked farro and cook, undisturbed, until grains are listening and toasted, 3 to 5 minutes. Add liced garlic, roasted parsnip, chopped red peppers, and lupini beans and cook until warmed through, 3 to 4 minutes. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Tear remaining basil leaves. Divide toasted farro mixture between large bowls. Add roasted broccolini and drizzle with basil cashew sauce. Top the Calabrian bean and veggie bowls with torn basil leaves and remaining white sesame seeds. Dig in! Let's Talk about Lupini Beans Lupini beans, a legume related to chickpeas and lentils, are popular in Mediterranean cisines. Because they have a slightly bitter taste, these beans are often pickled and eaten as a snack. They have a thick skin that you can eat, or you can squeeze the lupini bean and the seed will shoot out!

recipes.title: Thai Tempeh Khao Soi
recipes.subtitle: with Bok Choy & Crispy Onions
recipes.summary: with Bok Choy & Crispy Onions

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the rice noodles. Peel and mince the ginger and garlic. Trim and thinly slice the baby bok choy. Peel and thinly slice the shallot. Halve and juice the lime. Thinly slice the Thai chile.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the liced shallot, just half the lime juice, just 1 tsp turbinado sugar, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl and toss to combine.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Measure ¾ cup coconut milk and set aside. Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Add minced ginger, minced garlic, and red curry paste, and cook until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. Add reserved coconut milk, tamari, 1½ cups water, ½ tsp salt, and ½ tsp pepper. Bring to a simmer, add sliced baby bok choy, and reduce heat to low. TIP: Keep any remaining coconut milk for your own use.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add rice noodles to the large pot of boiling water, stir, and remove from heat. Cook, stirring occasionally, until noodles are tender, 8 to 10 minutes. Drain noodles and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Crumble tempeh and add to the skillet. Add a pinch of salt and pepper and cook, tossing occasionally, until tempeh is browned and crispy, 5 to 7 minutes. Add remaining turbinado sugar and cook another 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Add the remaining lime juice to the broth. Taste, and add salt as necessary. Divide broth between shallow bowls. Add the cooked rice noodles, crispy tempeh, pickled shallot, and sliced Thai chile. Top the Thai tempeh khao soi with crispy onions. Bon appétit!

recipes.title: Chile Butter Roasted Delicata Squash
recipes.subtitle: with Garlicky Greens & Lemon Yogurt
recipes.summary: with Garlicky Greens & Lemon Yogurt
ingredients: ¼ cup Forager Project® Organic Dairy-free Cashewmilk Yogurt

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Rinse and sort the French green lentils, and add to a small saucepan with 2¼ cups water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender, 18 to 20 minutes. Drain any remaining water. TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally occurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Trim the ends off the delicata squash, remove the seeds, and slice squash into 6 rounds, each about ½½ inch thick. Add sliced squash to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp vegetable oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until fork-tender, 18 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Zest and halve the lemon, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Add yogurt, just ½ tsp lemon zest, just 2 tsp lemon juice, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl, and stir. In another small bowl, combine butter and as much chile garlic sauce as you'd like, and mix well.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Roughly chop the gai lan. Heat 2 tsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add liced garlic and cook until lightly browned in places. Add chopped gai lan and a pinch of salt and pepper and cook until tender, 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Pick the dill fonds from the stem. Spread chile butter on the roasted squash. Divide the French green lentils and garlicky greens between plates, and top with chile butter roasted delicata squash. Drizzle with lemon yogurt and sprinkle with dill fronds and remaining lemon zest. Serve with lemon wedges and dollop any remaining chile butter on top. Enjoy! TIP: The dill fonds are the wispy green leaves on the thick stems. Delicate and Delicious! Some people say delicata squash tastes like a mix of fresh corn and pumpkin. We say: Yum! Plus, delicata squash is high in beta-carotene and versatile. It's great boiled, sautéed, steamed, and roasted—and the skin is delicious roasted, too.

recipes.title: Creamy Tomato Bisque
recipes.subtitle: with Quinoa & Parmesan Roasted Cauliflower
recipes.summary: with Quinoa & Parmesan Roasted Cauliflower

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat oven to 400°F. Add the quinoa, ½ cup (1 cup) water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan over high heat. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and cook until the spirals burst and water is absorbed, 10 to 12 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Transfer the cauliflower florets to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until tender and browned in places, 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Peel and chop the garlic. Peel and roughly chop the shallot(s). Heat 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add chopped garlic and chopped shallot and cook until softened, 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the cashews, tomatoes, coconut milk, and ¼ cup (½ cup) water to the large pot. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 3 to 5 minutes. Carefully pour bisque into a blender and purée until smooth. Sprinkle tomato bisque with salt and pepper. TIP: Blend in batches for the 4 serving meal.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Tear the basil leaves. Toss roasted cauliflower with just 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) parmesan. Divide the creamy tomato bisque between large bowls. Top with quinoa and parmesan roasted cauliflower. Sprinkle with torn basil leaves and remaining parmesan. Soup's on! Avoid a Soup Explosion Hot liquids expand and can create a powerful vortex in your blender. Follow these tips to ensure your soup ends up in your bowls, not on your walls. Give the soup miture a few minutes to cool down. Fill half the blender jar with soup, add the bender lid, and remove the center part of the lid or open the spout to release steam. Hold a towel over the opening to prevent soup from escaping, and turn on the blender. Start on low and slowly increase the speed. Voilà!

recipes.title: Herb Sausages
recipes.subtitle: with Caraway Roasted Potatoes & Maple Mustard Dressing
recipes.summary: with Caraway Roasted Potatoes & Maple Mustard Dressing

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 450°F. Peel and roughly chop the carrots and onion(s). Trim and halve the Brussels sprouts. Halve the fingerling potatoes lengthwise. Remove sausages from the casing and halve lengthwise.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the chopped carrot, chopped onion, halved Brussels sprouts, halved fingerling potatoes, caraway seeds, 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a baking sheet. Toss to evenly coat, and roast until the vegetables begin to brown, 15 to 17 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Combine the Dijon mustard and maple syrup in a small bowl. Mix the maple mustard dressing.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Place halved sausages cut side down and cook until well browned and crisp, 3 to 5 minutes. Flip sausages and cook another 1 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Divide the caraway roasted vegetables and herbed sausages between large, shallow plates. Drizzle 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) maple mustard dressing on top, and serve any remaning dressing on the side for dipping. Enjoy! Caraway Power! If you've eaten rye or pumpernickel breads, you've tasted caraway seeds. These delicious seeds, which are part of the parsley family, add a mild anise flavor and a citrusy, peppery smell to all sorts of classic dishes and savory baked goods. In particular, caraway is very common in Austrian and German cooking, but you'll also find it in Middle Eastern dishes and in condiments like harissa. Besides being a popular culinary ingredient, caraway has been used for hundreds of years as a digestive aid. Research shows that these flavorful seeds can help rebuild your intestinal flora and yeast, acting as a natural probiotic.

recipes.title: Crispy Hearts of Palm Tacos
recipes.subtitle: with Avocado Salsa & Chipotle Ranch
recipes.summary: with Avocado Salsa & Chipotle Ranch
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® High Omega Vegan Ranch Salad Dressing

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat oven to 350°F for the tortillas. Trim, halve, and thinly slice the radishes. Trim, deseed, and thinly slice the jalapeño(s). Drain the hearts of palm and cut each piece in half crosswise. Gently flatten each piece with the palm of your hand. TIP: If you have a toaster oven or microwave, you can use either one to warm the tortillas instead.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Halve and juice the lime(s). Add just half the lime juice, green cabbage, Mexican oregano, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl. Toss the curtido slaw.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Halve the avocado(s), remove the pits), and dice the flesh. Transfer diced avocado to a medium bowl. Add sliced radish, sliced jalapeño, remaining lime juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss the avocado salsa. Add ranch dressing and as much of the chipotle morita powder as you'd like to a small bowl, and whisk the chipotle aioli until smooth. TIP: We suggest starting with ⅛ tsp (¼ tsp) chipotle morita powder.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add ¾ cup (1½ cups) vegetable oil to a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add white rice flour, cornstarch, and ⅓ cup (2z cup) water to a medium bowl, and whisk until smooth. Dip flattened hearts of palm pieces in the batter and gently place them in the hot oil, working in batches. Cook until crispy, 3 to 4 minutes per side. Transfer crispy hearts of palm to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: Work in batches for the 4 serving meal.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Wrap the corn tortillas in foil and warm in the oven, 4 to 5 minutes. Place warmed corn tortillas on a flat surface and divide the curtido slaw and crispy hearts of palm between them. Add the avocado salsa and drizzle with chipotle aioli. Dig in!

recipes.title: Pan Seared Broccolini
recipes.subtitle: with Mashed Sweet Potatoes & Coconut Turmeric Relish
recipes.summary: with Mashed Sweet Potatoes & Coconut Turmeric Relish

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the sweet potato. Add diced sweet potato to a small saucepan and cover with 1 inch of water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes. Drain and return cooked sweet potato to the saucepan, off heat. Add beluga lentils and 1 cup water to another saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until lentils are tender, 20 to 24 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Trim, deseed, and halve the Fresno chile, and mince one half. Peel and mince 2 tsp ginger. Halve and juice the lemon. Trim 1 inch off the bottom of the broccolini. TIP: Save the remaining Fresno chile and ginger for your own use.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add sliced garlic and cook until lightly browned, 3 to 5 minutes. Add minced Fresno chile, minced ginger, golden raisins, coconut, brown mustard seeds, and turmeric. Stir, and cook until aromatic, 1 to 3 ° minutes. Add lemon juice and transfer the coconut turmeric relish to a medium bowl. TIP: Add more Fresno chile if you prefer more heat.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Wipe the skillet clean and return to mediumhigh heat. Add 2 tsp olive oil, ¼ cup water, and a pinch of salt. Add broccolini, cover, and cook until water evaporates and broccolini is tender and crispy in places, 3 to 5 minutes. Flip and cook for an additional 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add butter to the cooked sweet potatoes and mash with a fork. Taste, and add salt as necessary. Divide the mashed sweet potatoes and cooked beluga lentils between large plates. Top with pan seared broccolini and coconut turmeric relish. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Crispy Fabcakes
recipes.subtitle: with Fresh Herb Salad & Creamy Lemon Dijon Dressing
recipes.summary: with Fresh Herb Salad & Creamy Lemon Dijon Dressing
ingredients: ¼ cup + 2 tbsp Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Peel the carrots, then chop into 1 inch pieces. Peel and mince the garlic. Halve the lemon, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Drain and rinse the chickpeas. Drain and roughly chop the hearts of palm. Trim and halve the radishes. Pick the dill fronds from the stems. Trim, deseed, and roughly chop the jalapeño. TIP: Wash your hands and cutting board well after handling the jalapeño.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the chopped carrot, halved radishes, 1 tbsp vegetable oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a baking sheet. Toss to coat, and roast until tender and browned, 18 to 20 minutes. TIP: It's okay to start roasting the carrots before your oven is fully preheated.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the minced garlic, lemon juice, just half the Dijon mustard, just 2 tbsp Vegenaise, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a large bowl. Whisk the lemon Dijon dressing

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the chickpeas, chopped hearts of palm, chopped jalapeño, remaining Dijon mustard, remaining Vegenaise, New England seasoning, just ½ cup panko breadcrumbs, ½ tsp salt, and ¼ tsp pepper to a food processor. Pulse until well combined but not finely chopped, 5 to 8 pulses. Form the fabcake mixture into six 2 inch wide patties.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add 2 tbsp vegetable oil to a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add remaining panko breadcrumbs to a plate and gently press fabcake patties into breadcrumbs, coating both sides. Add breaded fabcakes to the skillet and cook until golden brown, 5 to 8 minutes per side. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: Add more oil as necessary to crisp the fabcakes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the dill fronds and baby arugula between bowls. Top with roasted carrots and radishes, and crispy fabcakes. Drizzle with creamy lemon Dijon dressing and serve with lemon wedges. Dig in!

recipes.title: Penne Mushroom Florentine
recipes.subtitle: with Chickpea Pasta & Toasted Walnuts
recipes.summary: with Chickpea Pasta & Toasted Walnuts

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the pasta. Thinly slice the cremini mushrooms. Peel and mince the garlic and shallots). Add walnuts to a large nonstick skillet over medium heat and toast, shaking the pan frequently, until fragrant, 3 to 5 minutes. Transfer toasted walnuts to a plate. water, and wipe mushrooms clean with a damp kitchen towel.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium-high heat with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil. Once hot, add sliced cremini mushrooms and a pinch of salt and cook, tossing occasionally, 3 to 5 minutes. Add minced garlic, minced shallot, and a pinch of salt and pepper, and cook until softened, 2 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add pasta to the boiling water. Cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente, 3 to 5 minutes. Reserve 1 cup (2 cups) pasta water. Drain the pasta and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the butter and sherry wine to the skillet with the mushrooms and cook for 1 minute. Add bouillon cube(s) to the reserved pasta water, whisk, and add to the skillet. Bring to a boil and cook until slightly thickened, 2 to 3 minutes. Add baby spinach and cooked pasta to the skillet and cook until hot, 1 to 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Divide the penne mushroom florentine between large bowls and top with toasted walnuts. Buon appetito! Delicious, Healthy, and... Foamy? Chickpea pasta is gluten-free and packed with protein and fiber, plus it has fewer carbs than traditional pasta. This is definitely the vegan pasta we've been waiting for. BTW, it's totally normal to see foam when you boil chickpea pasta. To help reduce the foaming, fill your pot with more water than you need, and lower the heat. You can also add a splash of oil, and frequently stir the pasta.

recipes.title: Sesame Ginger Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Stir-Fried Greens & Toasted Peanuts
recipes.summary: with Stir-Fried Greens & Toasted Peanuts

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add 2 quarts water and 1 tbsp salt to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil. Chop gai lan into 1 inch pieces. Peel and mince 2 tsp ginger. Trim and thinly slice the mini sweet peppers. TIP: Save any remaining ginger for your own use.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the minced ginger, tamari, peanut butter, rice vinegar, just half the sesame oil, just half the chili garlic sauce, and ¼ hot water to a medium bowl and whisk the sesame ginger sauce. TIPS: The sauce will not be completely smooth. Keep any remaining chili garlic sauce for your own use.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the udon to the boiling water and cook until al dente, 6 to 8 minutes. Drain noodles and run under cold water to stop the cooking process. Heat the remaining sesame oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add peanuts and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until nuts are toasted and fragrant, 1 to 3 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to transfer the toasted peanuts to a small bowl, leaving the oil in the skillet.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return the skillet with sesame oil to high heat. Thinly slice the Swiss chard. Add chopped gai lan, sliced Swiss chard, and a pinch of salt and pepper to the skillet. Cook until bright green and tender, 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the cooked udon and sesame ginger sauce to the skillet with the greens. Cook, tossing frequently, until evenly coated and warmed through, 1 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the sesame ginger noodles and stir-fried greens between bowls. Top with sliced mini sweet peppers and toasted peanuts. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Coconut Lentil Soup
recipes.subtitle: with Caraway Roasted Vegetables
recipes.summary: with Caraway Roasted Vegetables

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Chop cauliflower florets into bite-size pieces. Peel carrots and slice into rounds. Add chopped cauliflower and sliced carrot to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil. Sprinkle with caraway seeds and a pinch of salt and pepper, and roast until tender and browned in places, 18 to 23 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and dice the onion(s). Peel and mince the ginger and garlic. Rinse and sort the red lentils. Thinly slice the scallion(s). TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally occurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add diced onion and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add minced ginger and minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Open the coconut milk and reserve just 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) coconut cream for garnish. TIP: The coconut cream is the thick, solid cream at the top of the can.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Measure 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) tomato powder and add to the large pot along with the curry powder. Stir to coat the vegetables. Add red lentils, 2½ cups (5 cups) water, and a pinch of salt and pepper, and bring soup to a boil. Lower heat to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender, 15 to 18 minutes. TIP: Keep any remaining tomato powder for your own use.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Once the lentils are tender, add remaining coconut milk and stir. Bring soup to a simmer. Taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Ladle the coconut lentil soup into large bowls and top with caraway roasted vegetables. Sprinkle sliced scallion on top and dollop with the reserved coconut cream. Bon appétit!

recipes.title: Black Pepper Tofu
recipes.subtitle: with Chinese Broccoli & Brown Rice
recipes.summary: with Chinese Broccoli & Brown Rice

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add the short-grain brown rice, 1½ cups (3 cups) water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until water is absorbed, 30 to 35 minutes. Fluff the cooked short-grain brown rice with a fork.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and mince the garlic, ginger, and shallot(s). Mince the bird's eye chile(s). Drain tofu, pat dry with a clean kitchen towel, and cut into 1 inch cubes. Chop scallions into 2 inch pieces. Thinly slice the celery. Roughly chop the Chinese broccoli. TIP: Make sure to wash your hands after prepping the chile(s).

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Place cubed tofu in a large bowl and toss with the cornstarch. Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add cubed tofu and cook, tossing occasionally, until browned in places, 5 to 8 minutes. Transfer the crispy tofu to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: If necessary, work in batches for the 4 serving meal.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium heat and add butter. Once melted, add minced garlic, minced ginger, minced shallot, and as much minced bird's eye chile as you'd like. Cook until shallot softens, 1 to 2 minutes. In a small bowl, whisk the tamari and brown sugar until sugar dissolves. TIP: We suggest starting with 1 tsp minced bird's eye chile.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the crispy tofu, chopped scallion, sliced celery, tamari mixture, and up to 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) pepper to the skillet. Cook until tofu is heated through, 3 to 4 minutes. TIP: We will use this skillet again in the next step.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the short-grain brown rice between bowls and top with the black pepper tofu. Add chopped Chinese broccoli and ¼ cup (½ cup) water to the skillet over high heat. Sprinkle with salt and cook until crisp-tender, 2 to 3 minutes. Serve Chinese broccoli on the side. Bon appétit! TEd Ron. rear g e

recipes.title: Crispy Potato Skillet
recipes.subtitle: with Snap Pea Slaw & Scallion Sour Cream
recipes.summary: with Snap Pea Slaw & Scallion Sour Cream

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Rinse and sort the beluga lentils, and add to a small saucepan with ½ cup water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer, covered, until lentils are tender, 20 to 24 minutes. Drain any remaining water. TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally occurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the red bliss potatoes to a large pot, cover with 1 inch cold water, and bring to a boil. Cook until potatoes are fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes. Drain and let cool.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Thinly slice the sugar snap peas and scallions. Halve and juice the lemon. Add sliced snap peas, just half the sliced scallion, lemon juice, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl. Toss the snap pea slaw and set aside. Slice the red bell pepper into thin strips. Thinly slice the jalapeño.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add remaining sliced scallion, sour cream and a pinch of salt to a small bowl. Stir to combine the scallion sour cream. Peel and mince the garlic.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Place cooled boiled potatoes on a cutting board and, using the palm of your hand, gently press each potato until the sides blister open. Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add smashed potatoes and cook until golden brown and crispy, 3 to 5 minutes per side. Add sliced red bell pepper, minced garlic, butter, and paprika, and cook another 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Taste the crispy potato skillet and add salt as necessary. Scatter cooked beluga lentils over the skillet. Serve family style, topped with snap pea slaw, scallion sour cream, and sliced jalapeño. Bon appétit!

recipes.title: Spicy Dan Dan Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Gai Lan & Smoked Nori Spice
recipes.summary: with Gai Lan & Smoked Nori Spice

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the udon. Wipe cremini mushrooms clean with a damp kitchen towel. Roughly chop the cremini mushrooms. Peel and mince the garlic and shallot. Trim 1 inch off the gai lan ends. Thinly slice the cucumber into rounds, and add sliced cucumber to a small bowl with just 1 tbsp chile garlic sauce.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the remaining chile garlic sauce, tahini, rice vinegar, tamari, and ⅓ cup hot water to a medium bowl and mix.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the udon to the boiling water and gently stir. Cook until al dente, 5 to 7 minutes. Drain and run under cold water to stop the cooking process.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 2 tsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the gai lan and a pinch of salt and pepper and cook until gai lan is bright green and tender, 3 to 4 minutes. Transfer the sautéed gai lan to a plate and cover to keep warm.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium-high heat and add 2 tbsp vegetable oil. Add chopped cremini mushrooms, minced garlic, minced shallot, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until the mushrooms are browned, 4 to 5 minutes. Remove from heat, add the cooked udon and sauce to the skillet, and toss.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Halve the lemon and divide one half into wedges. Divide the dan dan noodles between plates and serve with the gai lan and spicy cucumbers. Sprinkle with smoked nori spice and squeeze the lemon half over each plate. Serve with lemon wedges. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Mexican Cobb Salads
recipes.subtitle: with Avocado & Cilantro Lime Dressing
recipes.summary: with Avocado & Cilantro Lime Dressing
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Dice sweet potato(es) into 1 inch pieces. Peel and chop the red onion(s) into 1 inch pieces. Transfer diced sweet potato and chopped red onion to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) vegetable oil, cumin seeds, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until sweet potatoes are tender, 18 to 22 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and mince the garlic. Finely chop the cilantro leaves and stems. Halve and juice the lime(s). Add minced garlic, chopped cilantro, lime juice, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl and mix the cilantro lime dressing

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Slice the corn tortillas into thin ¼ inch thick strips. Place tortilla strips on a baking sheet and toss with 2 tsp (4 tsp) vegetable oil and a pinch of salt. Bake until crispy and lightly browned, 5 to 6 minutes. TIP: Toss the tortilla strips halfway through baking.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Drain and rinse the black beans. Trim and chop the baby romaine lettuce. Trim and thinly slice the radishes. Halve the avocado(s), remove the pit(s), and dice the flesh.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Divide the chopped baby romaine lettuce between large plates and top with the black beans, roasted vegetables, sliced radish, and diced avocado. Drizzle Mexican Cobb salads with cilantro lime dressing and sprinkle with crispy tortilla strips. Dig in! The Most Polarizing Herb? Cilantro has been around for centuries. In fact, evidence of cilantro seeds was found in King Tut's tomb! Today you can find cilantro in Middle Eastern, North African, European, and Asian cuisines. But despite cilantro's inclusion in so many meals, its taste is very controversial; people tend to either love it or hate it. For those of us who love it, we can't get enough of the fresh, citrusy taste. However, for about 20 percent of the population cilantro will taste like soap. Why? It's a genetic predisposition. Fun fact: Julia Child famously despised cilantro.

recipes.title: Spicy Thai Coconut Corn Chowder
recipes.subtitle: with Fresh Chile & Lime
recipes.summary: with Fresh Chile & Lime

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Cut the corn kernels off the cobs). Peel and dice the onion(s). Deseed and dice the red bell pepper(s). Dice the Yukon Gold potatoes into ½ inch cubes. Halve the lime(s), juice one half (1 lime), and divide the other half (1 lime) into wedges. Mince the Thai chile(s). TIP: Be sure to wash your hands after preparing the chile.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) vegetable oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add diced onion, diced red bell pepper, and just half the minced Thai chile. Cook until onion is translucent and softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add green curry paste and cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. TIP: If you're sensitive to spice, use less Thai chile.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the corn, diced Yukon Gold potato, coconut milk and 2 cups (4 cups) water to the pot. Bring to a boil, and reduce heat to low. Simmer chowder until potatoes are fork-tender and coconut milk has thickened slightly, 8 to 10 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Thinly slice the scallions. Pick and roughly chop the cilantro leaves. Add lime juice to the chowder. Taste, and add salt as necessary. Ladle the spicy Thai coconut corn chowder into large bowls. Top with sliced scallion, chopped cilantro, and any remaining minced Thai chile. 4 Serve with lime wedges. Enjoy! Hot, Tasty, and Nutritious Red Thai chile peppers aren't HOT hot, but they definitely pack a punch. For reference, these peppers are about 23 times hotter than a jalapeño, and 3 times less spicy than a habanero pepper. These peppers are healthy, too. They've got vitamins A and C, which can help boost the immune system, as well as capsaicin, a chemical compound that contains anti-inflammatory properties and has been shown to help stimulate the circulatory system.

recipes.title: Black Bean Falafel
recipes.subtitle: with Avocado & Lime Aioli
recipes.summary: with Avocado & Lime Aioli
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Drain and rinse the black beans. Peel and mince the garlic. Peel and finely dice the onion. Slice the cucumber into rounds. Zest, halve, and juice the lime. TIP: If you have a Microplane® grater, you can also use that to grate the garlic.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: In a small bowl, combine just ¼ tsp minced garlic, just ½ tsp lime zest, just 2 tsp lime juice, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt. Mix the lime aioli well.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the black beans, remaining minced garlic, ½ cup diced onion, garbanzo bean flour, zhoug seasoning, and ½ tsp salt to a large bowl. Mix with your hands, crushing the beans, until dough is sticky and uniform. Form dough into approximately six patties, each about 2 inches wide. TIP: You can use a food processor for this step if you'd prefer.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil to a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add black bean falafel and cook until crisp and heated through, 3 to 5 minutes per side. TIP: Add more oil as necessary to crisp the falafel.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the baby spinach, remaining lime juice, 2 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a large bowl and toss. Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and thinly slice the flesh. Divide the baby spinach between bowls and top with black bean falafel, sliced cucumber, and sliced avocado. Drizzle with lime aioli. Dig in! TIP: If you'd like, top with remaining diced onion. Garbanzo Bean Flour Are you cooking with garbanzo bean flour yet? If not, let us convince you to try it! This flour is packed with protein, fiber, and vitamins, plus it's gluten free. And while some gluten-free flours don't hold up when baked, garbanzo bean flour has a sturdy yet tender texture that's perfect for quick breads, muffins, and fritters. Make it a staple in your kitchen!

recipes.title: Gnocchi
recipes.subtitle: with Dilly-Fried Cabbage & Apple Arugula Salad
recipes.summary: with Dilly-Fried Cabbage & Apple Arugula Salad

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the gnocchi. Peel and thinly slice the onion. Pick and chop the dill fronds. Halve and juice the lemon. Thinly slice the apple and add to a medium bowl with the baby arugula. TIP: The dill fronds are the wispy green leaves on the thick stems.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Melt the butter in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Once melted, add sliced onion and cabbage and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and softened, 5 to 7 minutes. Add smoked paprika and 1 tsp salt, stir, and reduce heat to low. TIP: While cooking the vegetables, reduce heat as necessary to prevent burning.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the gnocchi to the boiling water and cook until they float to the surface, 3 to 4 minutes. Drain, and add boiled gnocchi to the skillet with the cabbage. Cook, stirring occasionally, until browned in places, 4 to 6 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add just half the chopped dill and a pinch of pepper to the gnocchi. Taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary. Add remaining chopped dill, just half the lemon juice, sour cream, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl and combine.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add 2 tsp olive oil, remaining lemon juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper to the apple arugula salad. Divide the gnocchi with dillyfried cabbage between large plates and top with dill sour cream. Serve with apple arugula salad. Dig in! Global Sensation Cabbage is a big part of some of our favorite sides and condiments. Can you imagine a world without coleslaw, kimchi, or sauerkraut? Neither can we! We also love it fried, sautéed, and steamed. Bring on the cabbage! Not only is it tasty, it's also a nutrient powerhouse. A single serving of cabbage contains nearly half the recommended daily dose of Vitamin C, not to mention high levels of manganese, iron, vitamin B6, and fiber.

recipes.title: New England Lobster Rolls
recipes.subtitle: with Hearts of Palm & Lemon Pepper Potato Wedges
recipes.summary: with Hearts of Palm & Lemon Pepper Potato Wedges
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Slice Yukon Gold potatoes into ½ inch thick wedges and place on a baking sheet. Toss with 2 tsp (4 tsp) vegetable oil, a pinch of salt, and ¼ tsp (½ tsp) pepper. Roast potatoes wedges until crisp in places and lightly browned, 20 to 25 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Drain the hearts of palm and pat dry with a clean kitchen towel. Transfer to a baking sheet and gently toss with 1 tsp (2 tsp) vegetable oil, New England seafood seasoning, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Place pieces cut side down and roast until lightly browned, 18 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Peel and mince the shallots). Trim and thinly slice the celery. Finely chop dill fronds. Zest and halve the lemon(s), juice one half (1 lemon), and divide the other half (1 lemon) into wedges. Add minced shallot, sliced celery, chopped dill, just 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) lemon juice, Vegenaise, and capers to a large bowl. Mix the dill dressing. Taste, and add salt and pepper 3 as necessary.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Brush the top of each hot dog bun with 1 tsp olive oil and place oiled-side down in the skillet to toast, 1 to 2 minutes. TIP: You can also toast hot dog buns in the oven.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add lemon zest to the roasted potato wedges and toss to coat. Add roasted hearts of palm to the dill dressing and gently toss until evenly coated. Divide mixture between the toasted hot dog buns. Serve the New England lobster rolls with lemon pepper potato wedges and lemon wedges on the side. Bon appétit! Sea Seasoning Even when you're eating plant-based meals, there's just something about the allure of foods from the sea—especially in the summer! Our famous lobster rolls transform hearts of palm into something truly spectacular, with some help from New England seasoning. Made with salt, paprika, garlic, lemon peel, onion powder, red pepper, bay leaves, and mustard, this seasoning is light, sweet, and a little bit briny. Yum!

recipes.title: Kimchi Tofu Stew
recipes.subtitle: with Bok Choy & Sticky Rice
recipes.summary: with Bok Choy & Sticky Rice

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add the sushi rice, 1 cup water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until the grains are tender and water is absorbed, 15 to 18 minutes. TIP: For best results, rinse your sushi rice in a fine mesh strainer before cooking.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Cut the tofu into 1 inch cubes. Thinly slice the scallions, separating the white and green parts. Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Peel and mince the ginger. Cut the baby bok choy in half lengthwise.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat a large pot over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add sliced scallion whites, sliced garlic, and minced ginger and cook, stirring frequently, 1 to 2 minutes. Add cubed tofu and cook until crispy, 4 to 5 minutes. Remove the crispy tofu from heat and set aside.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return pot to stove over medium-high heat and add the bouillon cube, gochujang, tamari, and 2 cups water, and whisk to combine. Bring to a boil, and reduce heat to a simmer. Add halved baby bok choy and simmer until bright green and tender, 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add kimchi to the pot and stir. Taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary. Divide the stew between large, shallow bowls, and top with crispy tofu. Top the kimchi tofu stew with bok choy with sliced scallion greens and serve with sticky rice. * HW6LE, which means "enjoy your meal" in Korean! It's Soup Season! Yep, you heard us right! When you drink or eat foods that are hotter than your internal body temperature you trigger your body's eccrine glands, which release sweat to cool you downmaking this a great meal to keep you cool this summer! BTW, this same thing happens when you eat spicy foods. So, no time like the present to eat some (spicy) soup!

recipes.title: Sprouting Broccoli Salads
recipes.subtitle: with Sunflower Seeds & Miso Power Dressing
recipes.summary: with Sunflower Seeds & Miso Power Dressing

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Add the farro and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and cover with 1 inch cold water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and cook until farro is tender, 18 to 20 minutes. Drain farro and return to the saucepan, off heat.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and mince the garlic. Chop broccoli florets into bite-size pieces. Thinly slice Brussels sprouts. Trim and thinly slice mini sweet peppers into rounds. Check Castelvetrano olives and remove any pits. Halve the olives. Zest, halve, and juice the lemon.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic, chopped broccoli florets, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook, tossing occasionally, until broccoli is lightly browned and crisp-tender, 4 to 6 minutes. Turn off the heat, add lemon zest, and toss.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add 2 tbsp lemon juice, hemp seeds, white miso paste, ¼ cup water, and 2 tbsp olive oil to a blender. Blend the miso power dressing on high until smooth. Taste, and add salt as necessary.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the seared broccoli florets, sliced Brussels sprouts, 3 tbsp miso power dressing, and sunflower seeds to a large bowl. Toss the sprouting broccoli salad to combine.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the cooked farro between bowls and top with sprouting broccoli salad. Drizzle with remaining miso power dressing. Top with sliced mini sweet peppers and halved Castelvetrano olives. Bon appétit!

recipes.title: Creamy Tahini Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Black Lentils & Crispy Shiitakes
recipes.summary: with Black Lentils & Crispy Shiitakes

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Rinse and sort the beluga lentils. In a small saucepan, combine the lentils and 1¼ cup water. Bring to a boil, and reduce heat to low. Cook, covered, until the lentils are tender, 20 to 25 minutes. Drain any remaining water. TIP: To "sort" means to look for and discard any naturally occurring debris or stones.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Destem the kale and thinly slice the leaves. Wipe the mushrooms clean with a damp kitchen towel, trim the stems, and thinly slice the caps. Peel and mince the garlic. In a medium bowl, whisk together the minced garlic, tahini, tamari, agave, sesame oil, just 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 2 tbsp hot water, and a pinch of salt. Mix the tahini sauce well.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until well browned, 5 to 6 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Once the water is boiling, add the soba noodles and gently stir. Cook until al dente, 2 to 3 minutes. Drain and run under cool water to stop the cooking process.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium-high heat and add 1 tsp vegetable oil and the sliced kale. Cook until wilted, 2 to 3 minutes. Remove the skillet from heat and add the cooked soba noodles, just ¼ cup of the cooked lentils, and the tahini sauce, and stir well.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Toss the pea shoots with the remaining rice vinegar and a pinch of salt, directly on the cutting board. Divide the creamy tahini noodles between large bowls and add the crispy shiitakes. Sprinkle with the remaining beluga lentils and pea shoots. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Fiesta Enchilada Skillet
recipes.subtitle: with Mole Black Beans & Avocado
recipes.summary: with Mole Black Beans & Avocado

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the onion. Peel and mince the garlic. Trim and dice the zucchini. Drain and rinse the black beans. Cut the corn kernels off the cob.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add diced onion, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper, and cook until softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add diced zucchini, black beans, tomatoes, Mexican mole spice blend, cumin, and ¼ tsp salt, and cook until slightly thickened, 4 to 6 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Thinly slice the corn tortillas into ¼ inch thick strips. Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add tortilla strips and cook until lightly browned and crispy, 2 to 3 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt. TIP: The oil should sizzle when you add the tortilla strips.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Set the oven to broil on low. Add corn kernels to the skillet with the mole black beans and stir to combine. Top with mozzarella and place skillet in oven until cheese melts, 3 to 4 minutes. TIP: All broilers are different. Watch carefully to ensure cheese doesn't burn.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and slice the flesh. Halve the lime, juice one half, and divide the other half into wedges. Top the fiesta enchilada skillet with crispy tortillas, sliced avocado, and lime juice. Serve with lime wedges. Enjoy! Mole: A Labor of Love In Puebla, a small community about 85 miles south of Mexico City where mole is said to have originated, women start preparing the sauce several days before holidays and celebrations. They combine chiles, sour ingredients like tomatillos, sweet elements such as raisins or chocolate, and spices like anise and coriander for the sauce's base. Then the sauce is thickened with nuts, burnt tortillas, or even animal crackers. By using our mole spice blend, we skip the hard work while keeping the flavor in this dish!

recipes.title: Charred Pepper Ragout
recipes.subtitle: with Rotini & Crispy Artichokes
recipes.summary: with Rotini & Crispy Artichokes

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil for the pasta. Peel the garlic. Trim, deseed and chop the red bell peppers) and yellow bell peppers). Trim and roughly chop the red Fresno chile(s). TIP: If you prefer more garlic, add another clove.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Position a rack in the middle of the oven and set oven to broil on high. Place peeled garlic, chopped bell peppers, as much chopped red Fresno chile as you'd like, grape tomatoes, 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil, ½ tsp (1 tsp) salt, and ½ tsp (1 tsp) pepper on a baking sheet and toss to combine. Broil until peppers are charred and tender, 8 to 10 minutes. TIP: All broilers are different. Watch carefully to ensure the vegetables don't burn. We'll also use this baking sheet again in step 4.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Drain and rinse the artichoke hearts. Add rotini to the boiling water and stir. Cook until al dente, 7 to 9 minutes. Reserve ½ cup (1 cup) pasta water and drain the pasta. TIP: We will use this pot again in step 5.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the charred vegetables to a food processor and pulse until well chopped. Taste the charred pepper ragout and add salt and pepper as necessary. Place artichoke hearts on the baking sheet and add 1 tsp (2 tsp) olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Broil until browned and crispy, 6 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return the large pot to medium heat. Set aside a quarter of the charred pepper ragout and bring the rest to a simmer in the pot. Add cooked rotini, reserved pasta water, and just 2 tsp (4 tsp) parmesan. Continue to cook until warmed, 3 to 5 minutes. Taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the charred pepper ragout with rotini between large bowls. Top with crispy artichoke hearts and remaining charred pepper ragout. Sprinkle with remaining parmesan. Bon appétit!

recipes.subtitle: with Pickled Jalapeños & Barbecue Fries
recipes.summary: with Pickled Jalapeños & Barbecue Fries
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Peel and slice the red onion into ½ inch thick rounds. Cut the russet potatoes into ½ inch thick wedges. Peel and mince the garlic. TIP: Try to keep onion rounds intact to allow for even roasting.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Place the onion rounds on a baking sheet and toss with 2 tsp vegetable oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Push onion rounds to one side, and add the wedged potatoes, 2 tsp vegetable oil, BBQ seasoning, and a pinch of pepper to N the other side of the baking sheet. Toss to coat. Roast until the barbecue fries are crispy and browned in places, 17 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 2 tsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Once hot, add the burgers and cook until well browned, 5 to 7 minutes per side. Remove burgers from heat, top with Chao slices, and cover until cheese melts, 3 to 5 minutes. Slice the potato buns and toast directly on the oven rack, 3 to 5 minutes. TIP: If you prefer, you can also grill the burgers on medium heat for 5 to 7 minutes per side.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the minced garlic, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a small bowl. Mix the garlic aioli until combined.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Spread some garlic aioli on the toasted potato buns. Top with the burgers, roasted onions, and jalapeños. Serve with barbecue fries and any remaining garlic aioli for dipping. Dig in! Smile and Say Cheese! This week we're psyched to partner with Field Roast® to offer you extra creamy slices. This plant-based cheese is made from filtered water, coconut oil, modified corn and potato starch, fermented tofu, sea salt, natural flavor, olive extract, and beta carotene. The result is tasty, bold, and satisfying! We love to melt Chao slices on plant-based burgers (obvi!), vegan chili, and in grilled cheese sandwiches. This cheese is also great paired with your favorite crackers or even on its own. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Spicy Red Curry Coconut Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Bok Choy & Carrots
recipes.summary: with Bok Choy & Carrots

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the udon. Peel the carrots, and continue to peel lengthwise to create "noodles." Peel and thinly slice the shallots). Chop the unpeeled ginger into large pieces. Peel and thinly slice the garlic. Trim and thinly slice the baby bok choy. TIP: Cut the ginger into large pieces so that it will be easier to remove later.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tsp (2 tsp) vegetable oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add sliced shallot, chopped ginger, and sliced garlic. Cook until ginger and garlic are slightly toasted, 1 to 2 minutes. Add red curry paste, coconut milk, turbinado sugar, and ½ cup (1 cup) water. Bring broth to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer until ready to serve.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Once the water is boiling, add udon and cook until al dente, 8 to 10 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Pick the mint leaves. Halve and juice the lime(s). Remove ginger from the broth and add sliced baby bok choy. Add ½ tsp (1 tsp) salt and let broth continue to simmer for about 1 minute. Remove saucepan from heat and add the lime juice.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Divide cooked udon and carrot "noodles" between bowls and top with spicy red curry coconut broth. Sprinkle with mint leaves. Enjoy! Red Curry Paste Curry paste is made of fresh ingredients that are ground together to create a thick pureé. Pastes are usually thinned with a liquid, like coconut milk, to create soups or sauces. In this red curry paste we combine dried red chilies, garlic, shallot, salt, lemongrass, sugar, makrut lime, galangal, and spices like coriander seeds, cumin, and cardamom. The flavor tends to be a bit less spicy-the paste contains fewer red chilies-but it definitely maintains a tasty, moderate spice level, with some sweetness.

recipes.title: Spicy Almond Butter Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Gai Lan & Yellow Squash
recipes.summary: with Gai Lan & Yellow Squash

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil for the udon. Trim the gai lan, roughly chop the leaves, and thinly slice the stems. Slice yellow squash(es) into rounds. Thinly slice the scallions. Peel the garlic. Halve the lime(s), juice one half (1 lime), and cut the other half (1 lime) into wedges.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-low heat and add all the peanuts. Toast until fragrant, 3 to 4 minutes, and transfer to a bowl. Add garlic, lime juice, almond butter, tamari, turbinado sugar, chile garlic sauce, ½ cup (1 cup) water, and a pinch of salt to a blender. Blend almond butter sauce on high until smooth. Add just ¼ cup (½ cup) toasted peanuts and pulse to combine. TIP: The peanuts are added last, and pulsed instead of blended, to give the sauce texture.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Once the water is boiling, add udon and gently stir. Cook until al dente, 6 to 8 minutes. Drain udon and run under cold water to stop the cooking process.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return skillet to medium-high heat with 2 tsp (4 tsp) vegetable oil. Add chopped gai lan leaves and sliced gai lan stems, sliced yellow squash, and a pinch of salt. Cook until gai lan is bright green and tender, 3 to 5 minutes. Reduce heat to low, add spicy almond butter sauce, and bring to a simmer.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Add the cooked udon and half the sliced scallions to the skillet with the vegetables, and stir. Taste, and add salt as necessary. Divide spicy almond butter noodles between bowls and top with remaining toasted peanuts and remaining sliced scallions. Serve with lime wedges. Dig in! Get to Know Gai Lan Gai lan, or Chinese broccoli, is a nutrient-rich veggie. It's full of calcium, vitamin K, potassium, vitamin A, fiber, and folate. Gai lan is used a good deal in Chinese cooking, but it's also prevalent in Korean, Vietnamese, and Thai dishes. Fun fact: When you combine gai lan and broccoli you get broccolini.

recipes.title: Gnocchi Al Pesto
recipes.subtitle: with Charred Green Beans & Lemon Zucchini
recipes.summary: with Charred Green Beans & Lemon Zucchini
ingredients: Substitutes: broccoli for green beans, yellow squash for zucchini

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the gnocchi. Cut green beans in half. Trim the zucchini(s) and peel lengthwise to make ribbons. Halve the lemon(s), juice one half (1 lemon), and cut the other half (1 lemon) into wedges.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Add the zucchini ribbons, lemon juice, Aleppo pepper flakes, and a pinch of salt to a medium bowl. Toss to combine.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add halved green beans and cook until browned in places, 3 to 4 minutes. Add ¼ cup (½ cup) water and reduce heat to low. Cook green beans until crisp-tender, another 2 to 3 minutes. Sprinkle charred green beans with salt and pepper, and transfer to a bowl. TIP: We will reuse this skillet in a later step.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the gnocchi to the large pot of boiling water and cook until they float to the surface, 3 to 5 minutes. Drain gnocchi and shake off any excess water.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Return skillet to medium heat with 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil. Add cooked gnocchi and sauté, undisturbed, until browned in places, 2 to 4 minutes. Return charred green beans to the skillet along with the basil pesto and toss to combine. Sprinkle gnocchi with salt and pepper. TIP: If the pesto is too thick, add 1 tablespoon warm water (or more, if necessary), to create a thinner consistency.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the gnocchi al pesto with charred green beans between shallow bowls. Serve with lemon zucchini and lemon wedges. Mangia!

recipes.title: Spring Vegetable Gnocchi
recipes.subtitle: with White Beans & Lemon Basil Butter
recipes.summary: with White Beans & Lemon Basil Butter

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Bring a large pot with 4 quarts water and 2 tbsp salt to a boil. Peel and mince the garlic and shallot. Peel and dice the carrots. Trim 1 inch off the asparagus and cut the remaining stalks into rounds. Thinly slice the basil. Zest, halve, and juice the lemon. Drain and rinse the butter beans.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the minced garlic, minced shallot, diced carrot, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook, stirring often, until the carrots are tender, 5 to 6 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Add the gnocchi to the large pot of boiling water and cook until they float to the surface, 3 to 5 minutes. Reserve ½ cup of the pasta water and drain the gnocchi. Shake off any excess water.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Return the skillet with the carrots to medium-high heat and add the pasta water and butter and cook until melted, 1 to 2 minutes. Add the asparagus rounds and cook until bright green, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the lemon juice and just half of the basil, taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary. Transfer the spring vegetables and lemon basil butter to a medium bowl and cover to keep warm.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Wipe the skillet and return to high heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Once hot, add the butter beans, cooked gnocchi, ½ tsp salt, and ¼ tsp pepper. Cook, undisturbed, until well browned, 3 to 4 minutes. Sprinkle with lemon zest and parmesan, toss, and cook for another 2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the spring vegetables and lemon basil butter between bowls and top with the gnocchi and butter beans. Sprinkle with the remaining sliced basil. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Potato Malai Kofta
recipes.subtitle: with Sautéed Spinach & Tomato Chutney
recipes.summary: with Sautéed Spinach & Tomato Chutney

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Peel and dice the potato into bite-size pieces. Add the diced potato to a medium saucepan and cover with 1 inch salted water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes. TIP: Don't drain the potatoes yet because we'll add vegetables in step 2.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and chop the onions). Peel and slice the garlic. Peel and mince 2 tsp (4 tsp) ginger. Peel and grate the carrots) on the largest side of a box grater. Once the potatoes are fork-tender, add the grated carrot and green peas to the saucepan with the potatoes and cook for 1 minute. Drain potato and carrot mixture and transfer to a large bowl.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Return the empty saucepan to medium heat with 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil. Add the chopped onion, sliced garlic, and minced ginger. Cook until the vegetables are softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add the tomato powder, curry powder, coconut milk, and ½ tsp (1 tsp) salt, and bring sauce to a simmer. Cook until malai sauce is slightly thickened, 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: Add the garbanzo bean flour and ¼ tsp (½ tsp) salt to the large bowl with the potato and carrot mixture, and lightly mash until there are only a few lumps. Form the potato kofta mixture into 6(12) patties about 1 inch thick.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tbsp olive oil. Add the potato kofta and cook until browned and crisp, 1 to 2 minutes per side. Transfer the seared potato kofta to a plate. TIP: If you're making the 4 serving recipe, cook the kofta in batches.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium heat with 1 tsp (2 tsp) olive oil. Once hot, add the spinach and a sprinkle of salt and pepper. Cook until wilted, 2 to 3 minutes. Transfer the malai sauce to a blender and blend on low until smooth. Divide the malai sauce between plates and top with sautéed spinach and potato kofta. Serve with tomato chutney for dipping. Enjoy! TIP: Vent blender (by removing the plastic center of lid) to avoid splashing

recipes.title: Eggplant Parmesan
recipes.subtitle: with Garlicky Tomato Sauce & Lemon Basil Couscous
recipes.summary: with Garlicky Tomato Sauce & Lemon Basil Couscous

steps.title: Step 1
steps.text: Preheat oven to 425°F. Trim the eggplant and cut lengthwise into 4 planks. Place on a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Bake until lightly browned on each side, flipping halfway through, 15 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel and mince the shallot and garlic. Zest and halve the lemon; juice one half and cut the other half into wedges. Pick and tear the basil leaves. Add just half the minced garlic, mozzarella, breadcrumbs, and 2 tsp olive oil to a small bowl. Toss to combine.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Heat a medium saucepan over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the minced shallot, remaining minced garlic, and Italian seasoning. Cook until fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the tomatoes, ¼ tsp salt, and a pinch of pepper, and reduce heat to medium-low. Simmer until slightly thickened, 5 to 7 minutes. Add 1¼ cups water and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat. Add the Israeli couscous, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until water is absorbed, about 10 minutes. Add the lemon zest, lemon juice, just half the torn basil, 2 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Stir the lemon basil couscous to combine.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Remove the baked eggplant from the oven and top with garlicky tomato sauce and breadcrumb mixture. Return to the oven and bake until breadcrumbs are golden brown, 4 to 6 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.text: Divide the lemon basil couscous between bowls and top with eggplant parmesan. Sprinkle with the remaining torn basil and serve with lemon wedges. Tuck in!

recipes.title: Roasted Sweet Potatoes
recipes.subtitle: with Spicy Cashew Sauce & Kraut
recipes.summary: with Spicy Cashew Sauce & Kraut

steps.title: Step 2
steps.text: Peel the garlic and add to a blender. Measure just ⅓ cup (⅓ cup) cashews and add to the blender along with the rice vinegar, agave, tamari, Aleppo pepper, 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) olive oil, and 3 tbsp (4 tbsp) warm water. Blend spicy cashew sauce until smooth. Taste, and add salt as necessary. TIP: If you prefer more garlic, add another clove.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.text: Thinly slice the Swiss chard stems and roughly chop the leaves. Halve the limes), juice one half (1 lime), and cut one half (1 lime) into wedges. Thinly slice the Thai chile(s). Place remaining cashews in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat and toast, 3 to 4 minutes. Transfer toasted cashews to a cutting board and roughly chop. TIP: Be sure to wash your hands after preparing the chile(s).

steps.title: Step 4
steps.text: When the sweet potatoes are done, heat the skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tsp (2 tsp) olive oil. Add the chopped Swiss chard leaves and sliced stems, and cook until just wilted, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the lime juice and a pinch of salt.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.text: Divide the sautéed Swiss chard between large plates. Top with roasted sweet potatoes and sauerkraut. Drizzle everything with spicy cashew sauce and sprinkle with toasted cashews and sliced Thai chile. Serve with lime wedges. Tuck in! Time for Kraut Sauerkraut is experiencing a bit of a renaissance. Made from fermented cabbage and spices, it has a slightly sour taste which makes it the perfect foil for foods like sweet potatoes. But kraut is more han a tasty condiment: It has numerous health benefits, too! It's chock-full of healthy probiotics, fiber, and iron, plus it has a significant amount of Vitamin A.

recipes.title: Greek-Style Cauliflower Steaks
recipes.subtitle: with Tzatziki Sauce and Mashed Yams
recipes.summary: Little touches of vinegar and garlic give mashed yams a depth of flavor similar to Greek skordalia, a delicious potato spread. While it's traditionally eaten with pita, you'll instead pair this

steps.title: Prepare The Cauliflower
steps.text: Set your oven to broil on high. Trim the leaves and about 1 inch of the stem off the cauliflower, leaving the whole head intact if possible. Slice the cauliflower, from the top down, into 1 inch thick steaks. On a baking sheet gently coat the steaks in 1 tbsp olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Place on the middle rack and broil until wellbrowned, about 18 to 24 minutes.

steps.title: Cook The Japanese Yam
steps.text: Peel the Japanese yam and chop into large pieces. Transfer to a small saucepan, cover with 1 inch water, and add a pinch of salt. Bring the yams to a boil and cook until fork tender, about 10 to 12 minutes. Drain, return to the saucepan, and cover to keep warm.

steps.title: Mix The Tzatziki Sauce
steps.text: Grate the cucumber on the largest side of a box grater. Squeeze out the excess water and add the grated cucumber to a medium bowl. Chop the dill fronds and add them to the bowl along with the Vegenaise, juice from half the lemon, and a pinch of salt. Stir well to combine and place in the refrigerator until you're ready to serve.

steps.title: Prepare The Toppings
steps.text: Place a small skillet over medium heat and add the pumpkin seeds. Cook until fragrant and toasted, about 4 to 6 minutes. Check the kalamata olives for any pits and then roughly chop. Thinly slice the radish.

steps.title: Mash The Yams
steps.text: With the back of a fork, mash the Japanese yams until they're relatively smooth. Grate the garlic into the saucepan using a microplane or the smallest side of the box grater. Add the white vinegar and a pinch of salt and stir to combine.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Thinly slice the remaining lemon half. Spread the mashed yams on large plates tzatziki sauce and sprinkle with kalamata olives, toasted pumpkin seeds, hemp seeds, and radish. Serve with lemon slices.

recipes.title: Seitan Gyro
recipes.subtitle: with Shaved Artichoke Salad and Tahini Yogurt
recipes.summary: One of the best parts about a gyro is the combination of flavors and textures. Crispy seitan, dusted with baharat, is paired with crispy artichokes and a smooth vegan tahini yogurt. Baharat is a traditional Middle Eastern blend with allspice, cloves, and cumin. It brings an authentic, warm flavor to the dish, and you can use as little or as much as you'd like depending on your affinity for spice. Wrap everything up in warm pita and enjoy!
ingredients: ¼ cup Kite Hill vegan yogurt

steps.title: Get Ready
steps.text: Chop the parsley leaves. Slice the tomato. Thinly slice the cucumber.

steps.title: Artichoke Salad
steps.text: Thinly slice the artichoke hearts, be careful, they can be slippery! Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil. Once hot, add the sliced artichokes and cook until browned in places, about 5 to 7 minutes. Let cool slightly, then add to a medium bowl along with the parsley, juice from half the lemon, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss well to combine.

steps.title: Crisp The Seitan
steps.text: Thinly slice the Sweet Earth seitan. Add the potato starch to a plate and dust each piece, shaking off any excess. Return the large skillet to medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil. Once hot, add the seitan, sprinkle with salt, and cook until crisp, about 3 to 4 minutes. Cook on the other side until crisp, another 3 to 4 minutes. Sprinkle with baharat spice and remove from the heat.

steps.title: Make The Tahini Yogurt
steps.text: In a small bowl mix together the Kite Hill vegan yogurt, cumin, tahini, 1 tbsp of juice from the remaining lemon half, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Stir to combine.

steps.title: Warm The Pita
steps.text: Place the whole wheat pita in the toaster or microwave to warm. You can also set the oven to broil on low and broil until warm.

steps.title: Build and Serve
steps.text: Place the crispy Sweet Earth seitan onto the warmed pitas and top with the shaved artichoke salad, sliced tomatoes, and cucumbers. Drizzle with the tahini yogurt sauce. Serve any remaining salad and vegetables on the side.

recipes.title: Crispy Eggplant Falafel Wraps
recipes.subtitle: with Lemon Hummus and Parsley Salad
recipes.summary: The best part about falafel is the crispy coating, and in this dish we've replicated it on hearty eggplant, shallow fried until golden brown. Topped with creamy lemon hummus, these wraps will surely satisfy. If you need to keep the eggplant warm while you finish cooking, simply pop it in the oven on a baking sheet.

steps.title: Prepare The Eggplant
steps.text: Slice the eggplant into ½ inch thick rounds. Sprinkle each side with a little salt and lay them on paper towels to draw out the moisture.

steps.title: Blend The Hummus
steps.text: Drain the garbanzo beans and add them to a food processor. Zest 2 tsp of lemon zest and add it to the food processor along with the juice from half the lemon and a pinch of salt and pepper. While blending on high speed, drizzle in the sesame oil and 2 tbsp water. Blend hummus on high until smooth, adding more water as necessary. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt.

steps.title: Make The Batter
steps.text: In a medium bowl, combine the garbanzo bean flour, cumin, coriander, ½ cup plus 2 tbsp cold water, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Whisk the batter well. Place the panko breadcrumbs on a large plate.

steps.title: Crisp The Eggplant
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat with 2 tbsp vegetable oil. Dip the eggplant into the batter, shake off the excess, then coat in the breadcrumbs. Lay the eggplant into the hot oil and cook until the bottom is browned, 2 to 3 minutes. Flip, add another 2 tbsp vegetable oil, and cook until browned on the second side. Transfer eggplant to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Prepare The Salad
steps.text: Set the oven to broil on low. Pop the naan onto the oven racks to warm for 1 minute. Pick the parsley leaves and tender stems and add to a medium bowl. Slice the sun dried tomatoes and add them to the bowl along with the juice from the remaining lemon half. Season with salt and pepper and toss parsley salad to combine.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Stir the lemon hummus and spread it onto the warmed naan. Add a few slices of crispy eggplant, top with the parsley salad, and fold. Serve any remaining filling on the side, and enjoy any extra hummus for dipping.

recipes.title: Kimchi Quesadilla
recipes.subtitle: with Radish Pico De Gallo and Lime Crema
recipes.summary: What's better than a crispy quesadilla filled with smooth black beans and bright kimchi? Nothing — except for that it takes less than 30 minutes to prepare. Crunchy radishes add a great bite to classic pico de gallo - feel free to chop them however you'd like to create texture.
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® sour cream

steps.title: Make The Pico De Gallo
steps.text: Dice the tomato. Peel and mince the shallot. Slice the radishes into thin sticks. Chop the cilantro leaves and tender stems. Combine everything in a medium bowl. Halve the lime and add the juice from half. Add a pinch of salt and stir radish pico de gallo to combine.

steps.title: Prepare The "Crema"
steps.text: Combine the Follow Your Heart® sour cream and remaining lime juice in a small bowl. Add a pinch of salt and stir the lime crema.

steps.title: Mash The Beans
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Pour ¼ cup of liquid from the can of beans into a measuring cup and drain the rest. Add the black beans and the reserved liquid to the skillet along with the Mexican chile spice. Cook beans until they are hot, about 5 to 7 minutes. Mash them into a spreadable paste and season with salt.

steps.title: Build The Quesadillas
steps.text: Drain any liquid from the kimchi and roughly chop. Lay the flour tortillas flat on a clean work surface and top half of each tortilla with the mashed beans, spreading them out. Top the beans with the kimchi, fold tortillas, and set aside.

steps.title: Crisp 'Em Up
steps.text: Wipe the skillet clean and return to medium heat with 2 tsp vegetable oil. Once the oil is hot, add the quesadillas. Cook until the bottom is lightly browned, about 2 to 3 minutes, then carefully flip to brown the other side; another 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Cut each quesadilla into 3 pieces and divide between plates. Serve with radish pico de gallo and lime crema. Enjoy! PURPLE | CAR

recipes.title: Fettuccine Alfredo
recipes.subtitle: with Mushroom Medley
recipes.summary: Most of us are familiar with four tastes: sweet, sour, salty, and bitter. But umami, the fifth taste, is savory. Our cashew and miso cream sauce is packed with umami flavor. You'll pour it over whole wheat pasta for a hearty, flavorful meal.
ingredients: 2 tbsp vegan parmesan Green Enchiladas recipe bag.

steps.title: Set The Stage
steps.text: Place a large pot of salted water on to boil. Place the cashews in a small bowl and add ½ cup hot tap water. Let nuts soak for at least 10 minutes.

steps.title: Prep The Vegetables
steps.text: Wipe the mushrooms clean with a damp paper towel. Thinly slice the caps and the stems. Chop the parsley leaves. Peel and thinly slice the shallot.

steps.title: Blend The Sauce
steps.text: Peel 1 clove garlic. Add the cashews and their soaking water to a blender. Add the garlic, white miso paste, oyster mushroom powder, and just 1 tbsp lemon juice. Blend alfredo sauce on high until smooth. Season with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Saute The Mushrooms
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until softened and browned, about 4 to 6 minutes. Add the shallots, reduce heat to medium-low, and cook, stirring often, for an additional 2 minutes.

steps.title: Cook The Pasta
steps.text: Add the fettuccine to the boiling water, stir, and cook until al dente, about 6 to 8 minutes. Reserve ½ cup of cooking water and drain pasta. Add the fettuccine to the skillet along with the alfredo sauce, reserved cooking water, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Reduce heat to low and simmer pasta, tossing occasionally, until sauce has thickened and pasta is well coated, about 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Finish Up
steps.text: Stir the chopped parsley and just half of the parmesan into the pasta. Divide fettuccine alfredo between large plates or bowls and top with remaining parmesan. Mangia!
