recipes.title: Dosa Lettuce Wraps
recipes.subtitle: with Spiced Chickpeas and Mango Chutney
recipes.summary: A dosa is an Indian wrap, similar to a crêpe. Here, we lighten up the dish by using tender butter lettuce leaves instead of a traditional wheat-flour wrap. Cumin, coriander, and paprika are just some of the aromatic elements you'll find in the tandoori paste included in this recipe.

steps.title: Boil The Potatoes
steps.text: Quarter the red bliss potatoes, add to a small saucepan, and cover with 1 inch water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork tender, about 12 to 15 minutes. Drain the potatoes.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the onion. Peel and mince the ginger. Drain and rinse the chickpeas and pat dry.

steps.title: Crisp The Chickpeas
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over high heat with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the chickpeas and cook, shaking the pan occasionally, until browned in places, about 4 to 6 minutes. Remove from heat, add 1 tbsp tandoori paste, and stir until the chickpeas are evenly coated. Transfer to a bowl and cover to keep warm.

steps.title: Build The Flavor
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium-high heat with 2 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the sliced onion and cook until softened and browned in places, about 2 to 4 minutes. Add the ginger and curry powder and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Transfer the onions to a plate and sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Add The Potatoes
steps.text: Clean the skillet and return to medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, and the potatoes. Cook, smashing potatoes a bit, until crisp in places, about 5 to 7 minutes. Season with salt. Separate the butter lettuce leaves. Zest half the lemon and then cut into wedges.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Add the lemon zest and cooked onions to the potatoes. Roughly chop the cilantro leaves and tender stems. To build your lettuce wraps, top each butter lettuce leaf with some of the crispy lemon potatoes and spiced chickpeas. Dollop with each mango chutney and yogurt. Serve with lemon wedges and the remaining tandoori paste.

recipes.title: Portobello Steaks
recipes.subtitle: with Vegetable Orzo Salad & Chimichurri
recipes.summary: The best trick to trim asparagus is to hold the ends of a spear with both hands and find the natural snapping point towards the bottom of the stalk. Line them up and cut the entire bunch at this point, discarding the woody ends. The roasted vegetables get mixed into orzo and everything gets topped with herby, citrusy chimichurri, a South American sauce known for its bright color and bold flavor- it's one of our all time favorites.
ingredients: Parchment paper for the Seitan Massaman Curry.

steps.title: Marinate The Mushrooms
steps.text: Bring a small saucepan of salted water to a boil. Preheat the oven to 400°F. In a large bowl, add the portobello mushrooms, balsamic vinegar, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss well to coat the mushrooms and let marinate while you prepare the remaining vegetables. Line a baking sheet with the parchment paper.

steps.title: Roast The Vegetables
steps.text: Trim the zucchini and dice into ½ inch cubes. Trim the tough ends from the asparagus and cut remaining stalks into ½ inch pieces. Peel and chop the red onion, reserving 2 tbsp for Step 3. Add the vegetables to half of the lined baking sheet and drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Add the marinated mushrooms to the other half. Roast until tender, about 15 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Make The Chimichurri
steps.text: Peel and mince 1 garlic clove. Mince the reserved 2 tbsp red onion. Finely chop the parsley and cilantro leaves and tender stems. In a medium bowl, add minced garlic, red onion, parsley, cilantro, red wine vinegar, 2 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Zest 1 tsp of the lemon into the bowl and stir chimichurri to combine.

steps.title: Cook The Orzo
steps.text: Add the orzo to the boiling water and stir. Cook until tender, about 8 to 10 minutes. Drain the orzo, return to the saucepan and mix in 1 tsp olive oil.

steps.title: Finishing Touches
steps.text: Once the roasted vegetables are tender, mix into the orzo. Slice the mushrooms into a few pieces. Cut the lemon into wedges.

steps.title: Time To Eat
steps.text: Divide the vegetable orzo salad between your plates and top with the portobello steaks. Drizzle with chimichurri and serve with the lemon wedges. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Vegetable Chow Mein
recipes.subtitle: with Sweet Potato and Garlic Tamari Sauce
recipes.summary: This dish is our twist on a takeout favorite. Classic chow mein can be loaded with preservatives, but we've made it healthier and tastier and by adding fresh vegetables and using a simple tamari and garlic sauce.
ingredients: 2 tbsp chile garlic sauce Matar recipe bag.

steps.title: Prep The Vegetables
steps.text: Fill a small saucepan with water and place over high heat to boil. Peel the sweet potato with a peeler then continue peeling beyond the skin to make noodles. Peel and mince 1 clove garlic. Thinly slice the mushrooms. Deseed and thinly slice the red bell pepper. Trim the bok choy and roughly chop.

steps.title: Begin To Cook
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add sliced mushrooms and cook until browned in places, about 4 to 6 minutes. Add the sweet potato noodles, garlic, and red bell pepper. Cook until pepper is tender, about 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Cook The Noodles
steps.text: Add the noodles to the boiling water and immediately stir. Reduce heat to medium and cook until just al dente, about 2 to 3 minutes. Drain and rinse with cool water to halt the cooking.

steps.title: Sauce It Up
steps.text: Add the agave, tamari, and ¼ cup water to the skillet, and reduce heat to medium. Cook until the sauce thickens slightly, about 1 minute. Add the bok choy and water chestnuts to the skillet and toss to combine. Cook until bok choy is bright green and just tender, about 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Add The Noodles
steps.text: Add the cooked noodles to the skillet with the vegetables, sprinkle with salt, and gently toss them with tongs or a fork until well combined. Let noodles simmer, tossing occasionally to heat through, about 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Chow Down
steps.text: Divide vegetable chow mein between large plates and top with the spicy chile garlic sauce. Grab your chopsticks and dig in!

recipes.title: Mediterranean Eggplant
recipes.subtitle: with Cubanelle Chermoula and Apricot Couscous
recipes.summary: Chermoula is a marinade hailing from Northern Africa. It has a smoky, citrus-y taste. Here, we use it to complement the roasted texture of the eggplant and the sweetness of apricot couscous.

steps.title: Prep The Vegetables
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Cut the eggplant into rounds on the diagonal. Deseed the cubanelle pepper and roughly chop.

steps.title: Roast The Eggplant
steps.text: Add ¾ cup water to a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Add the whole wheat couscous, cover, and remove from heat. Let couscous stand until Step 5. Transfer eggplant to baking sheet and coat with 4 tbsp olive oil. Season generously with salt and pepper and place in the oven. Roast until eggplant is browned on the bottom and soft, about 30 to 35 minutes.

steps.title: Char The Pepper
steps.text: Place a medium skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the chopped cubanelle to the skillet. Cook, turning occasionally, until charred and softened, about 5 to 7 minutes. Remove from the heat to cool.

steps.title: Make The Chermoula
steps.text: Peel and grate the ginger. Grate 1 clove garlic into a medium bowl and add the ginger. Chop the dill fronds and add to the bowl. Zest the lemon and add just 1 tsp lemon zest to the bowl. Add the smoked paprika, cumin, ¼ tsp salt, 2 tbsp olive oil, and the juice from half the lemon. Finely chop the charred cubanelle pepper, add to the bowl and stir to combine.

steps.title: Finish The Couscous
steps.text: Finely chop the dried apricots. Fluff the cooked couscous with a fork and add the apricots, currants, lima beans, remaining lemon zest and juice, and a pinch of salt. Stir to combine.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Spread the vegan yogurt on the bottom of large plates. Top with apricot couscous, roasted eggplant, and cubanelle chermoula. Enjoy! OT

recipes.title: Elote Bowl
recipes.subtitle: with Lime Chickpeas & Quinoa
recipes.summary: Elote is the Spanish word for corn, and this sweet summer vegetable is the highlight of our "street corn" bowl. Mexican style corn is usually grilled, so fire it up if you have one. Instead of cutting the corn kernels from the cob, lay them whole on a hot grill and cook until charred, it should only take a few minutes. The key to crispy chickpeas is not shaking the pan too often, they should be browned in places and smell a bit toasted.

steps.title: Cook The Quinoa
steps.text: Add the red quinoa, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and place over high heat. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook quinoa until water is absorbed and spirals start to burst, about 15 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Drain and rinse the chickpeas, and dry them well with a paper towel. Peel the husks from the corn and cut the kernels off of the cob.

steps.title: Make The Elote Sauce
steps.text: Zest the lime. In a small bowl, combine half the lime zest, Kite Hill vegan yogurt, Vegenaise®, and half of the cayenne pepper. Add the juice from half of the lime and a pinch of salt. Stir elote sauce to combine.

steps.title: Prep The Garnishes
steps.text: Deseed and mince the jalapeño. Thinly slice the radishes. Finely chop the cilantro leaves and tender stems.

steps.title: Crisp The Chickpeas and Corn
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over high heat with 2 tsp olive oil. Once hot, add the chickpeas and cook undisturbed until slightly browned and crisp, about 4 to 6 minutes. Transfer to a medium bowl. Immediately return the skillet to high heat and add 2 tsp olive oil. Add the corn, cover, and cook, shaking the pan occasionally, until blackened in spots, 3 to 4 minutes. Sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Build The Bowls
steps.text: Add the juice from the remaining lime half, lime zest, jalapeño, and a pinch of salt to the crispy chickpeas, and toss well to combine. Divide the red quinoa between large bowls. Layer on the lime chickpeas, charred corn, and drizzle with elote sauce. Top with sliced radishes, cilantro, pumpkin seeds, and vegan parmesan. Sprinkle bowls with remaining cayenne pepper. Dig in!

recipes.title: Eggplant Parmesan Flatbread
recipes.subtitle: with Shaved Zucchini and Arugula Salad
recipes.summary: Mozzarella and sun dried tomatoes make this dish taste like an authentic eggplant parmesan. For slicing vegetables, a mandoline may be the most useful tool in your kitchen so if you have one, use it here for the eggplant and zucchini. If you like to grill, this is a good recipe to fire it up for. The eggplant can be grilled and after the flatbreads are assembled, simply leave them on the grill for a few moments with the lid closed to melt the cheese.
ingredients: ½ cup Follow Your Heart vegan mozzarella

steps.title: Cook The Eggplant
steps.text: Trim and thinly slice the eggplant lengthwise. Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and add 2 tbsp olive oil. Once hot, add the eggplant in one layer and cook until softened and browned, about 2 minutes each side. Once cooked, season with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Build The Flatbread
steps.text: Set the oven to broil on low. Spread the roasted garlic on the naan with a spoon. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and then layer on the eggplant, overlapping slightly, until all of the slices are used.

steps.title: Add The Toppings
steps.text: Pick the thyme leaves from the stems. Sprinkle the flatbreads with thyme leaves and sun dried tomatoes. Top with Follow Your Heart vegan mozzarella. Place the flatbreads onto a baking sheet. Broil until the vegan mozzarella is melted, about 4 to 6 minutes.

steps.title: Make The Salad
steps.text: Use a peeler or mandoline to make long ribbons of zucchini and add to large bowl. Roughly chop the hazelnuts. Add the chopped hazelnuts, arugula, white balsamic vinegar, and 1 tsp olive oil to the zucchini and season with salt and pepper. Toss salad well to combine.

steps.title: Cut The Flatbreads
steps.text: Once the flatbreads are finished, cut into quarters.

steps.title: Bon Appetit
steps.text: Serve the eggplant parmesan flatbreads on large plates alongside the shaved zucchini and arugula salad.

recipes.title: Curried Hummus Flatbread
recipes.subtitle: with Roasted Cauliflower & Olive Date Relish
recipes.summary: Buttery, briny, and tangy, these trendy Castelvetrano olives are named in honor of their hometown in Southwestern Sicily. The sweet dates add a counterbalance, and everything is piled high on crisp flatbread, dolloped with vegan ricotta and drizzled with lemon juice. You'll only use half of the can of garbanzo beans here, so be sure to save the remainder for the "Fabcakes" that also came in your box this week.

steps.title: Roast The Cauliflower
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Cut the cauliflower into even smaller florets. Transfer to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast the cauliflower until tender and browned in some places, about 15 to 18 minutes.

steps.title: Make The Hummus
steps.text: Halve the lemon. Drain and rinse the garbanzo beans. Measure out ¾ cup of garbanzos (reserving the remainder for the Fabcakes recipe). Add to the food processor along with the tahini, curry powder, and juice from half the lemon. With the motor running, slowly add 2 tbsp olive oil and blend until smooth, scraping down the sides as necessary with a spatula. Season hummus with ⅛ tsp salt.

steps.title: Naan Time
steps.text: Place the naan in the oven directly on the rack. Bake until lightly crisp, about 7 to 9 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Relish
steps.text: Finely chop the parsley leaves, Castelvetrano olives, and dates. Add to a small bowl and stir relish to combine.

steps.title: Assemble The Flatbread
steps.text: When the naan is toasted, spread each piece with half of the curried hummus. Layer on the olive date relish, roasted cauliflower, and dollop with bits of the vegan ricotta. Pop the flatbreads in the oven to warm, about 3 minutes.

steps.title: Finishing Touches
steps.text: Remove the flatbreads from the oven and top with arugula. Sprinkle with salt and drizzle with remaining lemon juice and 1 tsp olive oil. Bon appetit!

recipes.title: Sweet-and-Sour Brussels Sprouts Stir-Fry
recipes.subtitle: with Black Quinoa and Mint
recipes.summary: You'll char hearty Brussels sprouts in a skillet to develop flavor before tossing them in a sweet-and-sour sauce. The sauce has a little heat from the togarashi, a blend of chile, sesame seeds, ginger, and seaweed. Edamame amps up the protein and adds texture in this quick, delicious take on fried rice. tree nuts & soy.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Peel and mince the ginger. Peel and dice the carrot. Trim and halve the Brussels sprouts.

steps.title: Cook The Quinoa
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the minced ginger and diced carrot. Cook until fragrant, about 1 to 2 minutes. Add the black quinoa along with 1¼ cups water, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until all of the water is absorbed, about 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Make The Sauce
steps.text: Thinly slice half of the mint leaves. In a small bowl, combine the sliced mint, agave, rice vinegar, togarashi, and ½ tsp salt. Pick the remaining mint leaves, tear them with your hands, and set aside.

steps.title: Sear The Sprouts
steps.text: Place a medium skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the halved Brussels sprouts and a pinch of salt. Arrange sprouts cut side down and cook, undisturbed, until browned and crispy, about 2 to 5 minutes. Toss, lower heat to medium-low, and add the sauce. Toss once more, and cook until sauce is syrupy and sprouts are tender, about 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Finish The Quinoa
steps.text: Once quinoa is cooked, remove the lid and increase heat to high. Stir in edamame, sesame oil, and tamari. Cook, stirring occasionally, until edamame is warmed through and no liquid remains, about 3 to 4 minutes. Taste quinoa and season with salt as necessary.

steps.title: Plate and Serve
steps.text: Roughly chop the tamari almonds. Divide the quinoa between large shallow bowls and top with sweet-and-sour Brussels sprouts. Garnish with tamari almonds and torn mint. Chow down!

recipes.title: Kale Caesar
recipes.subtitle: with Crispy Butter Beans and Everything "Bagel" Croutons
recipes.summary: Fun fact: Caesar Cardini, an Italian immigrant, is credited with inventing the Caesar salad in the 1920's. Usually dressed with a combination of cheese, anchovies, egg, and oil, our kale Caesar takes a lighter approach, bumping up the flavor with briny capers, tahini, and dijon mustard. We also sub out runof-the-mill croutons for "everything bagel" croutons. You'll have trouble not eating them right off the baking sheet!

steps.title: Massage The Kale
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Destem the Lacinato kale and roughly chop the leaves. Place the kale in a large bowl with ½ tsp olive oil. Massage the kale with your hands until the leaves begin to soften, about 1 to 2 minutes.

steps.title: Make The Dressing
steps.text: Mince the capers and add to a small bowl. Add the tahini, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, dijon mustard, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Zest the lemon and set the zest aside. Add the juice from the lemon to the bowl and whisk Caesar dressing to combine.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the shallot. Trim and thinly slice the asparagus on the diagonal. Add the shallot and asparagus to the bowl with the massaged kale. Drain the butter beans and pat dry with a paper towel. Finely chop the parsley leaves.

steps.title: Make The Croutons
steps.text: Cut the whole wheat roll into cubes. Add to a baking sheet and toss with 2 tsp olive oil and everything bagel spice. Bake until toasted and crispy in places, about 7 to 9 minutes.

steps.title: Crisp The Butter Beans
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat and add 2 tsp olive oil. Once hot, add the butter beans and cook, shaking the pan occasionally, until browned in places, about 2 to 4 minutes. Season with salt and stir in the lemon zest and chopped parsley.

steps.title: Finish and Serve
steps.text: Toss the massaged kale with the Caesar dressing and a pinch of salt and pepper. Place a heaping serving of the kale Caesar on your plates. Top with the crispy butter beans and everything bagel croutons. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Wicked Healthy: Beetroot Tacos
recipes.subtitle: with Avocado Salsa
recipes.summary: Beets are high in Vitamin C and fiber, and make the perfect centerpiece in this tasty and healthy taco dish. You'll roast the beets until they're surprisingly sweet and chewy. Blending the salsa (rather than chopping the ingredients) saves time, and this version is particularly delicious.
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Vegenaise®
4 whole wheat tortillas all recipes this week.

steps.title: Roast The Beets
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Peel the red beets and slice them into thin wedges, about a half-inch thick. Place the beets on a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp vegetable oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until tender, about 25 to 30 minutes.

steps.title: Prep The Vegetables
steps.text: Destem the Lacinato kale and roughly chop the leaves. Halve and peel the red onion. Roughly chop half and thinly slice the other half. Halve the avocado and remove the pit. Trim and deseed the jalapeno.

steps.title: Make The Avocado Salsa
steps.text: Add the chopped red onion and half of the avocado to a food processor. Peel and add 2 cloves garlic, Follow Your Heart® Vegenaise, cilantro leaves and tender stems, just 1 tbsp of sherry vinegar, and as much or as little of the jalapeño as you'd like. Pulse until you have a smooth, even consistency, scraping down the sides as necessary. Season avocado salsa with salt.

steps.title: Cook The Kale
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the chopped kale and cook until just wilted, about 2 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Warm The Tortillas
steps.text: Once the beets are tender, drizzle them with the remaining sherry vinegar. Wrap the whole wheat tortillas in foil and warm in the oven for a few minutes. Slice the remaining avocado.

steps.title: Build Your Tacos
steps.text: Lay the warm tortillas on plates and top with a dollop of avocado salsa; spread to coat. Layer on the roasted beets, sautéed kale, sliced avocado, and some sliced red onion. Top with more avocado salsa and enjoy!

recipes.title: Cornmeal Arepas
recipes.subtitle: with Black Beans and Zucchini Citrus Salsa
recipes.summary: Arepas are tender, pan seared corn cakes made of masa harina (maize flour). The masa harina is finely ground flour traditionally usually used for tamales and corn tortillas. The dough will be slightly sticky when ready. Although delicious on their own, we've taken them to another level by adding a zucchini citrus salsa.

steps.title: Make The Arepa Mix
steps.text: In a large bowl, combine the masa harina, flour, vegan butter, turbinado sugar, ¼ tsp salt, and 1 cup hot tap water. Mix dough well with a wooden spoon to combine.

steps.title: Supreme The Orange
steps.text: Trim off the top and bottom of the orange with a knife. Set the fruit on end and cut the skin away. Over a medium bowl, cut out each section of the fruit by inserting the blade of the knife between the flesh and the membrane on both sides. Squeeze any remaining juice into the bowl from the membrane.

steps.title: Prepare The Salsa
steps.text: Peel, halve, and thinly slice the onion. Add as much or as little as you'd like to the medium bowl with the orange. Dice the zucchini. Thinly slice the radishes. Add the zucchini, radishes, 2 tsp olive oil, juice from half of the lime, and a pinch of salt and pepper to the bowl and toss salsa well to combine.

steps.title: Warm The Beans
steps.text: Drain and rinse the black beans. Place a small saucepan over low heat and add the black beans with ½ cup water. Cook, stirring occasionally, until they are warmed through, about 4 to 5 minutes. Mash beans with a fork and season with salt. Cover to keep warm.

steps.title: Make The Arepas
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil. To form the arepas, place about ¼ cup of the batter in your hands and roll it into a ball. Press gently into a flat circle and lay into the skillet. Cook the arepas until they are browned on the bottom and begin to firm up, about 5 minutes. Flip the arepas, add another 1 tbsp olive oil, and cook on the second side, about 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Serve It Up
steps.text: Cut the remaining lime half into wedges. To serve, spread the black beans on the cornmeal arepas. Top with some of the zucchini citrus salsa and mixed greens Serve the arepas with any remaining salsa and lime wedges. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Power Bowl
recipes.subtitle: with Rainbow Vegetables and Zesty Dijon Dressing
recipes.summary: Tender beluga lentils top this colorful mixture of raw and roasted vegetables, including beautiful and vitamin-rich broccoflower. The taste of crunchy vegetables is balanced out by a zesty Dijon dressing. We suggest you toss the salad with half and serve any remaining on the side.

steps.title: Simmer The Lentils
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Combine the beluga lentils and ⅔ cup water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and cook, covered, until lentils are tender and water has been absorbed, about 20 to 25 minutes. Drain any excess water and season with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Toast and Roast
steps.text: Add the walnuts to a baking sheet and bake until lightly toasted, about 4 to 6 minutes. Transfer the walnuts to a bowl. Cut the broccoflower into small florets and add them to the baking sheet. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and roast until tender and browned in places, about 7 to 9 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Thinly slice the radish. Peel the carrots with a peeler and then continue peeling to make ribbons. Slice the cucumber into thin rounds.

steps.title: Make The Dressing
steps.text: In a medium bowl, combine the Dijon mustard, agave, rice vinegar, yogurt, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Whisk the zesty Dijon dressing until smooth.

steps.title: Toss The Salad
steps.text: In a large bowl, toss the baby spinach, currants, a pinch of salt and pepper, and half of the zesty Dijon dressing.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Divide the dressed spinach between large bowls or plates. Top with sliced radish, carrot ribbons, sliced cucumber, beluga lentils, and roasted broccoflower. Drizzle with remaining zesty Dijon dressing and dig in!

recipes.title: Wicked Healthy Fajita Tostada
recipes.subtitle: with Chili-Lime Mango Salad
recipes.summary: Our friends at Wicked Healthy have come up with another stellar dish here and these spicy, sweet tostadas could be our new favorite. The trick to a crispy tortilla is to get an even, light coat of oil. Try using your hands if you don't have a pastry brush. We recommend that you bake the tortillas pressed between baking sheets to keep them flat, but if you'd prefer a more rustic look, by all means, leave them uncovered! To top off your dish, enjoy a Mexican classic, chili-lime mango salad.

steps.title: Cook The Lentils
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Add the red lentils, 1 cup water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, and reduce heat to low. Cook until the lentils are tender and all of the water has been absorbed, about 14 to 17 minutes.

steps.title: Crisp The Tostadas
steps.text: Lay the corn tortillas on a baking sheet. Brush both sides of each tortilla with ½ tsp vegetable oil and place another baking sheet on top to hold them down. Bake tortillas until golden brown and crisp, about 10 to 12 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Fajita Veggies
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the 5 cloves of garlic. Trim, deseed, and slice the mini sweet peppers. Peel and thinly slice the red onion. Place a large skillet over medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the garlic, sliced peppers and onion, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Adjust heat to low, and cook until vegetables are soft and caramelized, stirring occasionally, about 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Make The Mango Salad
steps.text: Zest the lime. Peel the mango, remove the flesh from the pit, and dice. In a large bowl combine the diced mango, lime zest, and the juice from half the lime. Sprinkle mango salad with a ½ tsp of the Mexican chili spice and toss to combine.

steps.title: Finish It Up
steps.text: Once the lentils are ready, mash them with a fork (they should be spreadable). Squeeze in the juice from the remaining lime half, taste, and season with salt and pepper. Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and slice the flesh.

steps.title: Serve and Enjoy
steps.text: Place the tostadas on large plates and top with the smashed red lentils. Layer on the fajita veggies, green cabbage, and avocado. Sprinkle with cilantro and remaining Mexican chili spice and serve with the chili-lime mango salad. Dig in!

recipes.title: Fabcake Sliders
recipes.summary: These cakes are fabulous, so we've nicknamed them "fabcakes" and popped them on slider buns. You'll use half of the garbanzo beans for this recipe, save the remaining garbanzos for the Curried Flatbread. The vegan remoulade, a creamy sauce often paired with seafood, adds a tangy and salty kick and pairs perfectly with the corn too.

recipes.title: Sweet Potato Lasagna
recipes.subtitle: with Tofu Ricotta and Marinated Bean Salad

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Peel the sweet potato and slice into thin half-moons, about ¼ inch thick. Peel and dice the shallot. Mince 2 cloves garlic. Finely chop the oregano leaves. Drain and rinse the cannellini beans.

steps.title: Start Your Sauce
steps.text: Place a medium oven-safe skillet over medium heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the diced shallot and a pinch of salt. Cook until the shallot is translucent, 1 to 2 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the crushed tomatoes, half of the chopped oregano, and a pinch of salt. Reduce heat to medium-low and bring sauce to a simmer.

steps.title: Simmer The Sweet Potatoes
steps.text: Add the sliced sweet potatoes to the sauce, cover with lid, and cook until tender you should be able to pierce the potatoes easily with a fork), about f0-to-42-minutes. Set your oven to broil on high. Z0 MIN

steps.title: Make The Tofu Ricotta
steps.text: Zest and halve the lemon. Drain the Wildwood® tofu and add it to a food processor along with ½ tsp chopped oregano, juice from half the lemon, 2 tbsp olive oil, ½ tsp salt, and ½ tsp pepper. Blend tofu ricotta until smooth. With a large spoon or spatula, evenly spread the tofu ricotta over the sweet potatoes and sauce. Broil lasagna until the top begins to brown, about 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Bean Salad
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Finely mince the remaining clove of garlic. Add garlic, cannellini beans, ½ tsp lemon zest, as many of the red chile flakes as you'd like, and ¼ tsp salt to the skillet. Cook until just warm, about 2 to 3 minutes. Squeeze juice from the remaining lemon half into the beans and add the baby kale. Toss well to combine.

steps.title: Serve and Eat Up
steps.text: Cut the sweet potato lasagna into pieces. Gently remove the pieces with a large spatula and place on large plates. Sprinkle with the remaining chopped oregano and serve with the marinated bean salad. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Za'atar Sweet Potato Noodles
recipes.subtitle: with Crispy Chickpeas and Broccolini
recipes.summary: In this simple and delicious meal, sweet potato noodles act as pasta. You'll toss them in a light lemon vinaigrette and season them with za'atar, a Middle Eastern spice blend of sumac, thyme, oregano, and sesame seeds. Thai basil leaves are the final touch, giving the dish another layer of flavor.
ingredients: 2 tbsp hemp seeds °Find a head of garlic in the Forest Bowl recipe bag.

steps.title: Set The Stage
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Place a large pot of salted water on to boil. Trim the broccolini and roughly chop. Drain the chickpeas, rinse, and pat dry with a paper towel.

steps.title: Prepare The Vinaigrette
steps.text: Zest the lemon over a medium bowl. Peel and grate the garlic into the bowl. Then add the juice from the lemon and the champagne vinegar. While whisking, slowly drizzle in 3 tbsp olive oil. Season vinaigrette with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Bake The Chickpeas
steps.text: Add the chickpeas to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp vegetable oil, 2 tsp of the za'atar spice, and dried mint. Season generously with salt and pepper and bake until chickpeas are crispy and lightly browned, about 14 to 16 minutes.

steps.title: Begin To Cook
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tsp olive oil. Once hot, add the broccolini and cook, tossing occasionally, until bright green and crisp-tender, about 4 to 6 minutes. Drop the sweet potato noodles into the boiling water and cook until al dente, about 3 to 4 minutes. Drain the noodles and add to the skillet with

steps.title: Add The Vinaigrette
steps.text: Add the vinaigrette to the skillet, sprinkle sweet potato noodles with salt and pepper, and toss to combine. Reduce heat to medium and cook for 2 minutes, or until heated through.

steps.title: Serve and Enjoy
steps.text: Hand tear the Thai basil leaves. Top the lemon za'atar noodles with crispy chickpeas, hemp seeds, and Thai basil. Sprinkle with remaining za'atar and serve lemon za'atar noodles family style. Dig in!

recipes.title: Spiced Chickpea Grain Bowl
recipes.subtitle: with Freekeh Tabbouleh and Roasted Beets
recipes.summary: Freekeh is not only a fun word to say, but there is also a great story behind each grain. The wheat that will become freekeh is actually harvested very young, roasted, and then sun dried. This technique gives the grain its distinctive chewy texture and nutty flavor, and it really shines in this herb packed tabbouleh. If you don't have two baking sheets, any oven safe casserole dish will work for roasting the beets and chickpeas.

steps.title: Cook The Freekeh
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Place a small saucepan over medium-high and add the freekeh. Toast in the dry pan, shaking occasionally, until the freekeh becomes fragrant, about 2 minutes. Add 1¾ cups water, a pinch of salt, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and let simmer until water has been absorbed, about 20 to 25 minutes. Remove from heat and let freekeh stand for an additional 5 minutes.

steps.title: Roast The Beet
steps.text: Peel the beet and thinly slice. Transfer to a baking sheet and toss with 2 tsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until beets are tender, about 15 to 18 minutes. Sprinkle the sesame seeds on the roasted beets and return to the oven until the seeds are golden and toasted, an additional 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Bake The Chickpeas
steps.text: Drain and rinse the chickpeas and pat dry with a paper towel. Add to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tbsp vegetable oil and berbere spice. Season the chickpeas generously with salt and pepper and bake until crispy and lightly browned, about 14 to 16 minutes.

steps.title: Prep The Tabbouleh
steps.text: Thinly slice the cucumber and the scallion. Halve the grape tomatoes. Roughly chop the mint and parsley leaves.

steps.title: Mix The Tabbouleh
steps.text: When the freekeh has finished cooking, add the cucumber, grape tomatoes, scallion, mint, and parsley to the saucepan. Stir in juice from half the lemon and 2 tbsp olive oil. Season freekeh tabbouleh liberally with salt and pepper. Cut the remaining lemon half into wedges.

steps.title: Plate
steps.text: Divide the freekeh tabbouleh between large bowls or plates. Top with sesame roasted beets and spiced chickpeas. Dollop with vegan yogurt and serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Wicked Healthy Chopped Salad
recipes.subtitle: with Charred Vegetables and Ginger-Miso Dressing
recipes.summary: This summer recipe comes from our good friends at Wicked Healthy, a Boston based Chef duo that want you to eat your vegetables and have them taste great. It can be easily adapted for a grill, so heat one up before you begin and forgo the skillet in Step 4. The garbanzo beans get pan toasted and dusted with a smoked nori spice: black and white sesame seeds with nori and smoky spices. Serve this meal family style lightly drizzled with dressing.
ingredients: 1 tbsp smoked nori spice* recipe is also for the Okonomiyaki.

steps.title: Prep The Grilled Vegetables
steps.text: Halve the savoy cabbage and reserve half for the Okonomiyaki recipe if you have not made it yet. Cut the remaining cabbage in half. Cut the zucchini into planks, about 3 to 4 inches long. Drain the garbanzo beans and dry well with a paper towel.

steps.title: Prep The Salad
steps.text: Destem and roughly chop the lacinato kale. Thinly slice the scallions and Thai chile. Pick the cilantro and mint leaves from the stems and roughly chop

steps.title: Make The Dressing
steps.text: Supreme the orange by trimming both ends and cutting away the peel and pith. Working over a medium bowl, remove the flesh segments, then squeeze out the remaining juice. Transfer the segments to a small bowl. Grate the ginger with a microplane (no need to peel) into the medium bowl with the orange juice and add the white miso paste and sesame oil. Whisk ginger-miso dressing to combine.

steps.title: Sear The Vegetables
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat and add 2 tsp olive oil. Once hot, add the cabbage and zucchini and season with salt and pepper. Reduce the heat to medium and cook, turning vegetables occasionally until charred in places and tender, about 4 to 6 minutes. Transfer to a cutting board

steps.title: Crisp The Garbanzos
steps.text: Return the skillet to medium-high heat and add 1 tsp olive oil. Add the garbanzos and cook, shaking the skillet frequently, until browned and crispy in places, about 2 to 3 minutes. Season with salt and add 1 tsp smoked nori spice.

steps.title: Build The Salad
steps.text: In a medium bowl, combine 1 tbsp gingermiso dressing with the kale and massage with your hands for about 1 minute. Chop the charred vegetables. Serve the salad on a large platter with the kale, chopped vegetables, and crispy garbanzos. Top with scallion, chiles, orange, herbs, and remaining smoked nori spice. Serve with ginger-miso dressing. Dig in!

recipes.title: Butternut Barley Bowl
recipes.subtitle: with Pickled Peppers and Pistachios
recipes.summary: To us, nothing says autumn like fresh herbs - especially rosemary and parsley. In this fall-inspired recipe, we use these herbs to add warmth to some hearty pearled barley and butternut squash. The sweetness of the squash is the perfect complement to the pickled peppers. This combination will leave your taste buds happy and your stomach satisfied.
ingredients: 2 oz baby arugula this recipe bag.

steps.title: Cook The Barley
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Place the barley in a small saucepan and cover with 2 inches of water. Bring to a boil, season with salt, and cook until tender, about 22 to 25 minutes. Drain barley and toss with 1 tsp olive oil to prevent sticking.

steps.title: Roast The Butternut
steps.text: Place the butternut squash onto a baking sheet and toss with 1 tsp olive oil. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and roast until tender and browned in places, about 18 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Pickle The Peppers
steps.text: Slice the mini sweet peppers into very thin strips, about ¼-inch thick. Add peppers to a small saucepan with the turbinado sugar, ¼ cup water, apple cider vinegar, pink peppercorns, and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat to low. Simmer until the peppers are soft and the liquid is syrupy, about 10 to 12 minutes.

steps.title: Chop The Herbs
steps.text: Peel and mince 1 garlic clove. Roughly chop the parsley leaves. Destem the rosemary and mince the leaves. Thinly slice the scallion.

steps.title: Toast The Pistachios
steps.text: Add the pistachios to a large skillet over medium heat. Cook until toasted and fragrant, about 3 to 4 minutes. Add the minced garlic and vegan butter to the skillet, and cook for an additional minute. Add the cooked barley, chopped parsley, rosemary, and scallions. Season with salt and pepper and toss to combine.

steps.title: Finish Up
steps.text: Stir the baby arugula into the barley mixture and divide between two large bowls. Top herbed barley with roasted butternut squash and pickled peppers. Bon appétit!

recipes.title: Cashew Korma
recipes.subtitle: with Cauliflower Rice
recipes.summary: The word "korma" means "to braise," and that's how the cashews and vegetables - red bell pepper, Yukon potatoes, and peas are cooked here. For this dish, we slowly cook the vegetables in coconut milk and layer in flavor with spices. This method mimics the effects of roasting, but cuts cook time in half.

steps.title: Make The Cauliflower Rice
steps.text: Peel the potatoes, dice, and add to a medium saucepan. Cover with 1 inch water, bring to a boil, and cook until tender, about 12 to 15 minutes. Reserve ¼ cup cooking water and drain potatoes in a colander. Chop the cauliflower into florets. In batches, pulse the cauliflower in a food processor, until it reaches an even, rice-like consistency.

steps.title: Cut The Veggies
steps.text: Peel and dice the onion. Peel and mince 1 garlic clove. Peel and mince the ginger. Deseed and chop the red bell pepper.

steps.title: Cook The Rice
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the cauliflower rice, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and cook, tossing occasionally, until it's just tender, about 5 to 7 minutes. Cover to keep warm. Drain and rinse the garbanzo beans.

steps.title: Start The Korma
steps.text: Return the large skillet to medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the onions and cook until slightly softened, 3 or 4 minutes. Add the Indian spice blend, garlic, ginger, bell pepper, cashews, and as much of the red chile flake as you'd like. Cook, stirring frequently until fragrant, about 1 minute.

steps.title: Simmer The Korma
steps.text: Add the cooked potatoes, garbanzo beans, coconut milk, peas, and reserved cooking water to the skillet. Bring sauce to a simmer, add the juice from half the lemon to the skillet, and reduce heat to low. Simmer korma, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is slightly thickened, about 4 to 6 minutes. Cut remaining lemon half into wedges.

steps.title: Serve and Enjoy
steps.text: Taste the korma and season with salt. Divide the cauliflower rice between bowls and top with cashew korma. Serve with lemon wedges. Dig in!

recipes.title: Taco Salad
recipes.subtitle: with Chipotle Seitan and Cashew Cheese Sauce
recipes.summary: Nacho cheese sauce that's packed with protein? Yes, please! Our cashew "cheese" sauce takes only a couple minutes to whisk up, and we do it last minute to ensure it's hot for serving. If you want to reheat, simply whisk in a bit warm water. If you prefer not to fry the tortilla strips, brush the tortillas lightly with oil before slicing and then roast them in the oven at 400°F until they are browned and crisp.

steps.title: Prepare The Toppings
steps.text: Remove the husk from the corn and cut the kernels off the cob. Thinly slice the radishes. Roughly chop the romaine.

steps.title: Crisp The Tortillas
steps.text: Place a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and add ½ cup vegetable oil. Slice the corn tortillas into thin strips. Once the oil is hot, add the tortilla strips and cook until golden brown and crisp, about 1 minute. Transfer to a paper towel lined plate and sprinkle immediately with salt. Transfer the hot oil to a heatproof container and return the skillet to medium-high heat.

steps.title: Cook The Seitan
steps.text: Add the Sweet Earth chipotle seitan to the hot skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned in places, about 4 to 5 minutes. Season seitan with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Build The Salad
steps.text: Place the chopped romaine on large plates and top with the corn, radishes, black olives, pickled jalapeños, and chipotle seitan. Halve the lime and squeeze half over the taco salads.

steps.title: Make The Cheese Sauce
steps.text: Add the cashew butter, nutritional yeast, turmeric, paprika, ½ tsp salt, and ½ cup cold water to a small saucepan. Bring sauce to a simmer while whisking continuously, until just thickened, about 30 seconds to 1 minute. Once thickened, add juice from remaining lime, stir, and remove from heat.

steps.title: Serve It Up
steps.text: Drizzle taco salads with some of the cashew cheese sauce and top with tortillas strips. Serve any remaining sauce on the side. Dig in!

recipes.title: Winter Squash Flatbread
recipes.subtitle: with Caramelized Apple and Rosemary
recipes.summary: Despite the rich flavors, this flatbread is remarkably light. You'll create a creamy "bechamel," or "white sauce," using vegan butter, almond milk, and a squeeze of lemon juice. The squash and apple toppings add a sweet note that offsets the bitter punch of arugula. You may have extra minced rosemary save it for cooking on your own.

steps.title: Roast The Squash
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Strip the rosemary leaves from the stems and mince the leaves. Add the squash to a baking sheet with 1 tsp vegetable oil, just 1 tsp minced rosemary. and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until the squash begins to brown in places, about 14 to 18 minutes. Peel and mince the shallot. Peel and mince 1 clove garlic.

steps.title: Make The Bechamel
steps.text: Place a small saucepan over medium heat and add the butter. Add the shallot and garlic and cook until softened, about 2 to 3 minutes. Add arrowroot starch and stir into a paste. Add the juice from half the lemon, almond milk, and a pinch of salt. Stir until thickened, about 1 minute, and remove from the heat.

steps.title: Caramelize The Apple
steps.text: Thinly slice the apple. Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Add the sliced apple and 1 tsp of the remaining minced rosemary to the skillet. Sprinkle with salt and cook, stirring frequently, until tender and browned in places, about 4 to 6 minutes. Squeeze in the juice from the remaining lemon onto the apples.

steps.title: Crisp The Naan
steps.text: Pop the naan into the oven and heat until it's a little crispy, about 5 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: Build The Flatbreads
steps.text: Spread the bechamel sauce onto the naan and top with the roasted squash and caramelized apples. Pop the flatbreads back into the oven for 3 minutes to warm. In a medium bowl, toss the arugula with 1 tsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper.

steps.title: Cut and Serve
steps.text: Top the winter squash flatbreads with some of the arugula salad. Drizzle with white balsamic vinegar and slice. Serve alongside any remaining arugula salad. Dig in!

recipes.title: Shakshuka
recipes.subtitle: with Poached Tofu and Crispy Kale Chips
recipes.summary: Middle Eastern shakshuka tends to be a breakfast dish, but it translates well to dinner. You'll poach creamy, protein-packed tofu instead of eggs. The sauce comes together fairly quickly, and will gain flavor as it simmers. When you're ready to dollop the tofu in, use a large, rounded spoon for best results.
ingredients: Fresh cilantro °Find a head of garlic in the Miso-Walnut Meatballs recipe bag.

steps.title: Make The Freekah
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Add the freekeh, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, and cook until all of the water is absorbed and the grains are tender, about 20 to 25 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables.
steps.text: Peel and slice the onion. Deseed and thinly slice the red bell pepper. Peel and slice the garlic. Destem the red kale and hand tear the leaves into large pieces.

steps.title: Start The Shakshuka
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tbsp olive oil. Add the sliced onion and red bell pepper, reduce heat to medium, and cook until soft, about 6 to 8 minutes. Add the garlic, cumin, paprika, and a pinch of the red chile flakes, and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the fire-roasted tomatoes and ½ cup water. Simmer until the sauce has thickened slightly, about 8 to 10 minutes.

steps.title: Make The Kale Chips
steps.text: Add the red kale leaves to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tsp olive oil. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Roast until crisp, about 8 to 10 minutes.

steps.title: Dollop The Tofu
steps.text: Taste and season the shakshuka sauce with salt and pepper. Open the silken tofu. Use a large spoon to gently scoop out dollops of tofu and drop them into the sauce. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Serve and Enjoy
steps.text: Chop the cilantro leaves and tender stems. Divide the freekeh between large plates and top with the shakshuka. Sprinkle with cilantro and serve with the crispy kale chips. Dig in!

recipes.title: Spicy Almond Butter Udon
recipes.subtitle: with Charred Brussels Sprouts
recipes.summary: Smooth almond butter blends into to a deliciously creamy sauce with the help of tart lime juice and sweet maple syrup. The citrus-spiked sauce coats crisp pan seared vegetables over udon, a thick Japanese noodle that can stand up to the toppings.

steps.title: Cook The Udon
steps.text: Place a large pot of salted water on to boil. Trim the Brussels sprouts and halve or quarter them. Peel the carrot and cut into 2 inch long sticks. Thinly slice the scallions. When the water comes to a boil, add the udon noodles and cook until al dente, about 6 to 8 minutes. Rinse noodles under cold water and toss with 1 tsp vegetable oil.

steps.title: Make The Sauce
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-low heat and toast the peanuts, about 4 to 5 minutes. Cool and roughly chop. Halve the lime. In a blender, combine juice from half the lime, garlic, almond butter, tamari, as much of the chili garlic sauce as you'd like, maple syrup, and ¼ cup water. Blend on high until smooth. Add half the peanuts, season with salt, and blend once more.

steps.title: Char The Brussels
steps.text: Place the same skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the Brussels sprouts and cook, shaking the pan often, until charred in places, about 3 to 5 minutes. Add ¼ cup water to create some steam, reduce heat to medium, and continue cooking until sprouts are bright green and just tender, about 3 to 5 more minutes.

steps.title: Get Saucy
steps.text: Add the carrots and continue to cook until tender, about 4 to 6 minutes. Reduce heat again to low and add edamame, almond butter sauce, and ¼ cup + 2 tbsp water. Toss vegetables to combine, reduce heat, and bring sauce to a simmer.

steps.title: Add The Udon
steps.text: Add the cooked udon to the skillet, taste and season, and toss to combine. Stir in half the chopped scallion.

steps.title: Finish It Up
steps.text: Divide spicy almond butter udon between large plates and top with remaining scallion and chopped peanuts. Serve with lime wedges. Dig in!

recipes.title: Vegetable Pad Thai
recipes.subtitle: with Toasted Peanuts and Lime
recipes.summary: We think you'll be pleasantly surprised at how quickly and easily this takeout favorite can come together in your own kitchen. Instead of a heavily sweetened sauce, we use natural lime juice and cashew butter. Pro tip for rice noodles: they cook quickly, so in Step 2, simply drop them in the boiling water and turn off the heat.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Place a large pot of salted water on to boil. Trim the bok choy and roughly chop. Cut the green beans in half. Deseed and thinly slice the red bell pepper. Chop the scallions into 2 inch pieces. Chop the cilantro leaves and tender stems.

steps.title: Cook The Noodles
steps.text: Once the water is boiling, add the rice noodles and turn off the heat. Stir the noodles occasionally until they are al dente, about 8 to 10 minutes. Drain the noodles and run them under cool water to stop the cooking process.

steps.title: Toast The Peanuts
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium heat and add the peanuts. Cook, tossing occasionally, until fragrant and toasted, about 4 to 6 minutes. Transfer the toasted peanuts to a small bowl.

steps.title: Sauté The Vegetables
steps.text: Return the large skillet to medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the bok choy, greens beans, red bell pepper, and scallions. Sprinkle with salt and cook, stirring frequently, until the bell pepper is tender and the bok choy and green beans are bright green, about 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: Add The Sauce
steps.text: In a medium bowl, whisk together the juice from the lime, cashew butter, tamari, ⅓ cup hot tap water, and as much of the sambal chile paste as you'd like. Add the sauce to the skillet with the vegetables, and then add the cooked rice noodles. Reduce heat to medium and cook, tossing frequently, until the noodles are heated through, about 2 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Divide the vegetable pad Thai between plates. Sprinkle with toasted peanuts and chopped cilantro. Pass any remaining sambal chile paste at the table.

recipes.title: Vietnamese Tofu Bowl
recipes.subtitle: with Bamboo Rice and Pickled Vegetables
recipes.summary: This bowl is highlighted by bright colors and bold flavors. Bamboo rice is infused with juice from fresh bamboo, which is where it gets its light green color. The combination of this fragrant rice and sweet and sour pickled vegetables is one of our favorites. We hope it becomes one of yours, too!
ingredients: 1 package WildWood® organic extra firm sprouted tofu
4 oz spring mix® with other recipes this week.

steps.title: Cook The Rice
steps.text: Combine the bamboo rice, 1 cup water, and a pinch of salt in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, and reduce heat to low. Cook until all the water is absorbed and the rice is tender, about 14 to 16 minutes. Remove from heat, and let stand for 10 minutes before fluffing rice with a fork.

steps.title: Prepare To Pickle
steps.text: Peel the carrot and then continue to peel lengthwise to create "noodles." Thinly slice the jalapeño.

steps.title: Pickle The Vegetables
steps.text: Add the turbinado sugar, rice vinegar, and ⅔ cup water to another small saucepan and bring to boil. Add the carrot, shredded daikon radish, and as much of the jalapeño as you'd like. Turn off the heat and move the saucepan to a cool spot on the stovetop.

steps.title: Prepare The Aioli and Tofu
steps.text: Add the potato starch and a pinch of salt to a plate. In a small bowl, mix together the Vegenaise and as much of the sriracha as you'd like. Drain the tofu and cut into 6 triangles (rectangular slices will also work). Wrap the slices in paper towels and press to drain the excess water.

steps.title: Cook The Tofu
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tbsp vegetable oil. Toss the sliced tofu in the potato starch and shake off any excess. Once the oil is hot, carefully lay the tofu into the skillet, and cook until crispy and lightly golden, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer tofu to a plate and sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Pick half of the cilantro leaves and tender stems (saving half for the Spicy Red Curry Ramen). Drain the liquid from the pickled vegetables. Divide the spring mix between large bowls. Top with bamboo rice, crispy tofu, pickled vegetables, and cilantro. Drizzle the Vietnamese tofu bowls with sriracha aioli and serve with any remaining sriracha for an extra, spicy kick. Dig in!

recipes.title: Walnut-Crusted Artichokes
recipes.subtitle: with Dijon Lentils and Green Beans
recipes.summary: Artichokes are best paired with creamy notes, and this walnut crust does the job. It will bubble on top, crisping in the the oven while you finish up the warm lentil salad. Roasted green beans are another highlight, and a simple side dish you can add to your repertoire. & soy.

steps.title: Cook The Lentils
steps.text: Add the French lentils, 2 cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan over high heat. Bring lentils to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer until tender, about 18 to 20 minutes. Drain any remaining water and transfer lentils to a medium bowl.

steps.title: Make The Walnut Crust
steps.text: Drain the artichokes and halve lengthwise. Finely chop the parsley leaves. Finely chop the walnuts. In a small bowl, combine the parsley, walnuts, Follow Your Heart® Vegenaise®, as much of the red chile flakes as you'd like, and a pinch of salt and pepper.

steps.title: Prepare The Vegetables
steps.text: Set the oven to broil on low. Place the artichokes on a baking sheet cut-side up and toss with 1 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Broil until crispy on the edges, about 10 to 12 minutes. Peel and mince the shallot. Trim the green beans. Chop the roasted red peppers.

steps.title: Finish The Lentils
steps.text: Once the lentils are cooked, add the minced shallot, roasted red peppers, champagne vinegar, Dijon mustard, and 2 tbsp olive oil. Season Dijon lentils with salt and pepper and stir to combine.

steps.title: Add The Green Beans
steps.text: Move the roasted artichokes to one side of the baking sheet. Spread the walnut crust over each of the artichokes. Add the green beans to the empty side of the baking sheet and toss only the green beans in any residual oil. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Broil until green beans are just tender and walnut crust begins to brown, about 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: Plate and Eat Up
steps.text: Zest the lemon onto the finished artichokes and green beans. Cut the lemon into wedges. Divide the Dijon lentils and green beans between plates and top with the walnut-crusted artichokes. Serve with the lemon wedges. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Potato Latkes
recipes.subtitle: with Fennel Slaw and Stone Fruit Preserves
recipes.summary: It's latke season! Although they are delicious on their own, you'll make them even better by pairing them with a creamy horseradish sauce and a crunchy fennel slaw. Stone fruit preserves are served on the side-a little bit of this sweet spread goes a long way.

steps.title: Grate The Potatoes
steps.text: Scrub the Yukon gold potato and grate with a box grater into a large bowl. Squeeze the grated potato to remove as much of the water as possible. Discard the liquid, wipe the bowl clean, and return the grated potato. Peel and finely dice the onion. Add just ½ cup of the diced onion and reserve the remaining diced onion for your own use.

steps.title: Prepare The Slaw
steps.text: Cut the fennel bulb in half lengthwise, remove the V-shaped core, and thinly slice the bulb. Trim the sugar snap peas and thinly slice. In a medium bowl, combine the sliced fennel, sugar snap peas, white balsamic vinegar, 1 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss until coated evenly, and set aside.

steps.title: Make The Sauce
steps.text: In a small bowl, combine the horseradish, just 2 tbsp of Follow Your Heart® Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt. Stir until well combined.

steps.title: Form The Latkes
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat with ¼ cup vegetable oil. To the grated potatoes, add the remaining Vegenaise, flour, thyme, 1 tsp salt, and ½ tsp pepper and stir until well combined. Use a ¼ cup measuring cup to divide the mixture into 6 equal portions, and form them into latkes about 3 inches in diameter.

steps.title: Cook The Latkes
steps.text: Once the oil is hot, carefully place each latke into the skillet, pressing down with the bottom of the measuring cup until each patty is about a half inch thick. Lower heat to medium and fry until golden brown and crispy, about 3 to 4 minutes per side. Transfer latkes to a paper-towel lined plate and season with salt.

steps.title: Plate and Enjoy
steps.text: Divide the fennel slaw between plates. Top with the potato latkes and serve with stone fruit preserves and horseradish sauce. Dig in!

recipes.title: Butter Roasted Tomato Gratin
recipes.subtitle: with Garlic Breadcrumbs and Toasted Farro
recipes.summary: A gratin is a dish with a crunchy, toasted topping, usually hiding a savory, creamy layer of deliciousness beneath. Here, you'll use kale, cannellini beans and garlicky breadcrumbs, with buttery roasted tomatoes as the base. The roasted garlic sea salt is a quick way to build flavor-adjust the seasoning level to your liking.

steps.title: Cook The Farro
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Add the farro to a medium saucepan and cover with 1 inch of water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer until grains are tender but still have a bit of "chew," about 18 to 20 minutes. Drain farro well and spread it out on baking sheet to cool.

steps.title: Roast The Tomatoes
steps.text: Mince the garlic. Combine the minced garlic, tomatoes, butter, just half of the thyme sprigs (keep the leaves on), and a pinch of salt and pepper in an 8x8 baking dish. Bake the tomatoes until the butter has melted, about 10 minutes. Open the oven, give the buttered tomatoes a stir, and return the dish to the oven to bake for an additional 15 minutes.

steps.title: Toast The Breadcrumbs
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium heat with 2 tsp olive oil. Add the panko breadcrumbs and a pinch of the roasted garlic sea salt. Toast the breadcrumbs, stirring frequently, until golden brown, about 2 to 4 minutes. Taste and add more roasted garlic sea salt if you'd like. Transfer the breadcrumbs to a small bowl. Pick the remaining thyme leaves from the stems and finely chop.

steps.title: Cook The Kale
steps.text: Destem the kale and roughly chop the leaves. Drain and rinse the cannellini beans. Return the large skillet to medium-high heat with 1 tsp olive oil. Once hot, add the chopped kale and reduce heat to medium. Cook the kale until bright green and tender, about 3 to 5 minutes. Add the cannellini beans and season with salt and pepper. Toss to combine, and cook until hot, about 1 minute.

steps.title: Toast The Farro
steps.text: Remove the thyme sprigs from the buttered tomatoes. Top the tomatoes with the kale and cannellini beans and sprinkle with breadcrumbs. Return the gratin to the oven until the top is well-browned, about 3 to 5 minutes. Wipe the skillet clean and return it to medium-high heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the farro and chopped thyme and cook until the grains are crisp and develop a golden sheen, about 5 to 7 minutes.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Divide the toasted farro between large plates and top with the butter roasted tomato gratin.

recipes.title: Tomato-Braised Artichokes
recipes.subtitle: with Bright Dill Sauce and Farro
recipes.summary: Artichokes are rich in vitamins and minerals-and they taste good, too. Farro, an ancient grain packed with antioxidants, serves as the perfect bed for your savory, saucy artichokes. You'll top off the dish with a drizzle of Greek-style dill sauce.
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Vegenaise®

steps.title: Cook The Farro
steps.text: Add the farro to a small saucepan, cover with 1 inch water, and bring to a boil. Cook until the grains are tender, about 18 to 20 minutes. Drain farro and toss with 1 tsp olive oil. Season with salt and pepper and cover to keep warm.

steps.title: Cut The Vegetables
steps.text: Peel and mince the shallot. Mince the garlic. Cut the artichoke hearts in half lengthwise. Check the olives for any pits, remove if necessary, and chop.

steps.title: Begin To Braise
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tsp olive oil. Add the shallot and garlic and cook until fragrant and slightly toasted, about 1 minute. Add the artichokes and stir to combine. Add the crushed tomatoes and 1 cup water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer artichokes until the sauce has thickened slightly and you're ready to plate.

steps.title: Prepare The Dill Sauce
steps.text: Grate the cucumber on the on the large side of a box grater. Squeeze out the excess water from the cucumber and add cucumber to a medium bowl. Finely chop the dill fronds. Add the dill, juice from half the lemon, Follow Your Heart® Vegenaise®, and a pinch of salt to the bowl. Stir dill sauce to combine.

steps.title: Add The Finishing Touches
steps.text: Cut the remaining lemon half into wedges. Season the artichokes with ½ tsp salt.

steps.title: Plating Time
steps.text: Divide the farro between plates and top with the tomato-braised artichokes. Drizzle with dill sauce and sprinkle with the chopped olives. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Black Bean and Squash Enchiladas
recipes.subtitle: with Pumpkin Seed Mole
recipes.summary: The term mole (MOE-lay) describes a wide variety of Mexican sauces. For this dish, you'll use a classic mole paste that includes ancho chiles, almonds, raisins, cocoa, and sesame. This notoriously labor intensive sauce has been simplified here, without sacrificing on flavor. nuts & soy.
ingredients: Find a head of garlic in the Forest Bowl recipe bag.

steps.title: Cook The Rice
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Combine the Spanish rice and ⅔ cup water in a small saucepan over high heat. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until the rice is tender and the water is absorbed, about 15 to 20 minutes. Peel and dice the onion. Peel and mince the garlic.

steps.title: Start The Mole
steps.text: Place a medium saucepan over mediumhigh heat with 2 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 3 to 5 minutes. Reduce heat to medium, add pumpkin seeds and cook, stirring constantly, until the seeds are lightly toasted, about 3 to 5 minutes. Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.

steps.title: Simmer The Mole
steps.text: Add 1¼ cups water and mole paste to the pumpkin seed mixture and stir until the paste dissolves. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and cook until sauce has thickened slightly, about 5 to 8 minutes. Drain and rinse the black beans. Deseed and thinly slice the red bell pepper.

steps.title: Prepare The Filling
steps.text: Place a large skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the butternut squash and cook until browned in places and tender, about 4 to 6 minutes. Add the sliced pepper and cook until just tender, about 1 to 2 minutes. Add the black beans, cooked Spanish rice, and ½ tsp salt and stir to combine.

steps.title: Roll The Enchiladas
steps.text: Transfer pumpkin seed mole sauce to a blender and blend until smooth. Add the juice from half the lime and season with 1 tsp salt. Spread ¼ cup of the mole on the bottom of a baking dish. Lay the tortillas on a clean work surface and top each with ½ cup of the vegetable rice mixture, mixing any remainder into the sauced baking dish. Drizzle each with 1 tbsp mole sauce and roll to seal, lying seam-side down into the dish.

steps.title: Plate and Serve
steps.text: Top enchiladas with remaining mole sauce, cover with foil, and bake until warmed through, about 8 to 10 minutes. Cut the remaining lime half into wedges. Thinly slice the scallion. Serve black bean and squash enchiladas on large plates and top with sliced scallion and lime wedges. Olé!

recipes.title: Pesto Bean Burgers
recipes.subtitle: with Roasted Peppers and Carrot Fries
recipes.summary: These white bean burgers are deliciously tender on the inside and crispy on the outside. The roasted red peppers and basil not only give the burgers a pop of color but great flavor and texture. You'll finish off the meal with tasty carrot fries.
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Pesto Vegenaise®

steps.title: Prepare The Mix
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Zest the lemon. Drain and rinse the cannellini beans. Add the lemon zest, beans, 2 cloves garlic, flaxseed meal, garbanzo bean flour, herbs de Provence, as much of the red pepper flakes as you'd like, and ¼ tsp salt to the food processor. Pulse a few times to mix, until just combined.

steps.title: Make The Patties
steps.text: Divide the bean mixture into two equal portions and form into patties about 1 inch thick. Place in the refrigerator until you're ready to cook them.

steps.title: Prep The Carrot Fries
steps.text: Peel the carrots and cut into 4 inch long sticks. On a baking sheet, toss with 2 tsp olive oil, coriander, salt and pepper. Bake carrot fries until tender and brown in places, about 10 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Sear The Burgers
steps.text: Place a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Cook the burger patties until golden brown and firm, about 4 to 6 minutes per side.

steps.title: Prepare The Trimmings
steps.text: Slice the roasted red peppers into thin strips. Pop the pretzel buns in the oven to warm. Pick the basil leaves from the stems.

steps.title: Build The Burgers
steps.text: Halve the lemon. Spread the top pretzel buns with some of the Follow Your Heart® Pesto Vegenaise® On the bottom buns layer the burger patties, roasted red peppers, basil, and baby kale. Top with a squeeze of lemon juice and close them up. Serve bean burgers with the carrot fries and any remaining Pesto Vegenaise for dipping. Dig in!

recipes.title: Pumpkin Matar
recipes.subtitle: with Coconut Yogurt and Brown Basmati Rice
recipes.summary: Bright tomato and creamy coconut milk deliver a delicious base for this seriously satisfying dinner. You'll toss tender roasted pumpkin into the sauce for an extra layer of flavor, and add some texture with a sprinkle of pumpkin seeds.
ingredients: 2 tbsp vegan yogurt recipe bag in all recipes this week.

steps.title: Cook The Rice
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Add brown basmati rice, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and cover. Cook until all water is absorbed and rice is tender, about 30 to 35 minutes.

steps.title: Roast The Pumpkin
steps.text: Add the cubed pumpkin to a baking sheet and toss with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and roast until tender and browned in places, about 18 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Produce
steps.text: Peel and chop the onion. Peel and mince or grate 1 clove garlic. Peel and mince or grate the ginger. Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once the oil is hot, add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add The Aromatics
steps.text: Add the garlic and ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the garam masala and toast for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.

steps.title: Add The Pumpkin
steps.text: Add the tomato paste, coconut milk, and ½ cup water to the skillet, and lower heat to medium. Simmer sauce until slightly thickened, about 4 to 6 minutes. Add the roasted pumpkin and green peas to the simmering sauce and season with salt and pepper. Stir to combine.

steps.title: Serve It Up
steps.text: Divide brown basmati rice between large plates or bowls and top with the pumpkin matar. Garnish with pumpkin seeds and yogurt. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Loaded Burritos
recipes.subtitle: with Tofu "Chorizo" and Spanish Rice
recipes.summary: The Spanish rice you'll use in these loaded burritos is packed with goodness, including paprika, bell peppers, celery, onions, and kaniwa (a grain similar to quinoa). There are so many delicious ingredients in this recipe that you may have trouble keeping all of the filling inside the burritos. Stuff them with what you can and serve any remaining beans and rice on the side.
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Chipotle Vegenaise®

steps.title: Cook The Rice
steps.text: Add the Spanish rice and 3 cup water to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low and cover. Cook until all of the water is absorbed and the rice is tender, about 15 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Prep The Vegetables
steps.text: Peel and dice the onion. Peel and mince 1 clove garlic. Drain and rinse the black beans. Drain the Wildwood® tofu and dry well with paper towels. Destem the Lacinato kale, keeping the leaves as intact as much as possible. Massage kale leaves gently with your fingers to soften them slightly. Thinly slice the roasted red peppers.

steps.title: Prepare The "Chorizo"
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the diced onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 4 to 5 minutes. Crumble the tofu into the skillet and add the Mexican oregano, Mexican chile spice, and ½ tsp salt. Cook until the tofu is browned in places and has dried out a little, about 7 to 9 minutes.

steps.title: Build The Burritos
steps.text: Lay the tortillas on a clean work surface. Top with Spanish rice, tofu chorizo, roasted red peppers, ¼ cup black beans, and some of the massaged kale. Roll the burritos, tucking in the sides as you go.

steps.title: Sear The Burritos
steps.text: Wipe the skillet clean and return to medium heat with 2 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the burritos to the skillet, seam side down, and cook until golden brown on the bottom, about 2 to 3 minutes. Carefully flip the burritos and cook an additional 2 minutes.

steps.title: Add The Finishing Touches
steps.text: In a small bowl, mix the Follow Your Heart® Chipotle Vegenaise® with the juice from half of the lime. Cut the other half into wedges. Slice burritos in half and serve with chipotle lime sauce, any remaining kale and black beans, and lime wedges.

recipes.title: Seitan Massaman Curry
recipes.subtitle: with Blistered Cherry Tomatoes & Brown Basmati Rice
recipes.summary: Massaman is a staple in many Thai restaurants, but the history can be traced back to the Middle East. The tangy tamarind base of the curry gets rounded out with warm aromatic spices like cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves. We sear the seitan before it gets tossed in the sauce so that it stays crisp, so don't be afraid to let it get nice and browned in the skillet. Adjust the heat as you go and shake the skillet frequently for best results.
ingredients: Fresh basil recipe bag is for all recipes in the box this week.

steps.title: Cook The Rice
steps.text: Add brown basmati rice, 1¼ cup water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and cook, covered, until all water is absorbed and rice is tender, about 30 to 35 minutes. Turn off the heat, leave covered for 5 minutes, and then fluff rice with a fork.

steps.title: Prep The Vegetables
steps.text: Peel and chop the onion. Peel and thinly slice 3 cloves of garlic. Trim the ends off the haricot vert. Halve the lime.

steps.title: Crisp The Seitan
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat and add 1 tbsp vegetable oil. In a medium bowl, toss the Sweet Earth seitan with the rice flour and a pinch of salt and pepper. Once the oil is hot, add the seitan to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally. until crisp in places, about 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: Cook The Vegetables
steps.text: Transfer the crispy seitan to a plate. Return the skillet to medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the cherry tomatoes, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes are blistered and begin to burst, about 2 minutes. Add the onion, garlic, and haricot vert to the tomatoes and cook until just softened, about 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add The Curry Paste
steps.text: Add the massaman curry paste to the vegetables and stir to combine. Add the coconut milk, ¼ cup water, and reduce heat to low. Simmer curry until the sauce has thickened slightly, about 5 to 10 minutes. Squeeze half of the lime into the skillet and stir. Taste the curry and season with salt and pepper. Stir the crispy seitan into the curry.

steps.title: Serve The Meal
steps.text: Cut the remaining lime half into wedges. Divide the brown basmati rice between large bowls and top with seitan massaman curry. Use your hands to tear the basil leaves over the bowls. Serve with lime wedges. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Collard Green Enchiladas
recipes.subtitle: with Salsa Roja and Roasted Potatoes
recipes.summary: Plant-based tortillas? Yes, please. Collard greens make a great substitute. Smothered in salsa roja (a classic Mexican red sauce), the tender rolls of chipotle "sausage" and vegetables will leave you satisfied.
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® vegan cream cheese
½ cup Follow Your Heart® vegan mozzarella Fettucine Alfredo recipe.

steps.title: Roast The Potatoes
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Quarter the red potatoes and add to a baking sheet. Toss with 1 tsp vegetable oil and generously season with salt and pepper. Roast until crispy on the bottom and you can easily pierce with a fork, about 18 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Cook The Collards
steps.text: Place a large pot with a few inches of salted water on to boil. Add the ancho chile to a small saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a simmer over medium heat. Trim the tough stems from collard greens; the leaves should stay intact. Once the water is boiling, add the leaves to the large pot and press into the water with a spatula. Cook collards until bright green, about 1 minute. Drain and rinse with cold water.

steps.title: Cook The Filling
steps.text: Peel and chop the onion. Deseed and chop the green bell pepper. Wipe the pot dry. return to medium-high heat, and add 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once hot, crumble the chipotle sausage into the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until crispy, 3 to 5 minutes. Add the chopped onion and pepper, and cook until vegetables are softened, about 4 to 5 minutes. Mix in the Follow Your Heart® cream cheese.

steps.title: Make The Salsa Roja
steps.text: Peel 3 cloves of garlic. To a blender, add the garlic, diced tomatoes, cumin, and ¾ cup water. Deseed the rehydrated ancho chile and add as much or as little as you'd like to the blender. Blend salsa until smooth, taste, and season with salt. Spread ½ cup of salsa roja onto the bottom of an 8x8 baking dish.

steps.title: Roll The Enchiladas
steps.text: Lay the collard leaves out on a work space and divide the sausage mixture between them. Roll, folding in the sides (like a burrito), and place enchiladas in the baking dish seam side down. Cover with salsa roja (you may have some left over) and sprinkle with the mozzarella. Bake the enchiladas, uncovered, until the top is browned and sauce is bubbling, about 10 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Plate and Enjoy
steps.text: Scoop the collard green enchiladas onto plates. Serve with roasted potatoes and any remaining salsa roja. Dig in!

recipes.title: Sweet Potato Sushi Bowl
recipes.subtitle: with Sambal Cucumbers and Yuzu Sauce
recipes.summary: Yuzu is an aromatic fruit, somewhere between a lemon, grapefruit, and mandarin. For this dish, you'll use yuzu juice, and the taste will take your sushi bowl to the next level. Kizami nori, a traditional Japanese garnish, is sprinkled on top. Try to get a little in each bite for maximum flavor.

steps.title: Roast The Sweet Potatoes
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Cut the sweet potato into wedges, transfer to a baking sheet, and toss with 2 tsp vegetable oil, salt, and pepper. Bake until the wedges are crisp and slightly browned, about 20 to 25 minutes.

steps.title: Cook The Rice
steps.text: Combine the quinoa speckled rice, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt in a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and cook, covered, until all of the water is absorbed and the rice is tender, about 15 to 20 minutes.

steps.title: Marinate The Cucumber
steps.text: Thinly slice the cucumber on the diagonal and place in a medium bowl. To the bowl, add the sambal, rice vinegar, tamari, and sesame oil. With a fork, toss the cucumbers with the sauce, ensuring they're evenly coated. Set aside to marinate.

steps.title: Prepare The Yuzu Sauce
steps.text: Combine the yuzu juice and Follow Your Heart® Vegenaise® in a small bowl. Sprinkle with salt and mix yuzu sauce to combine.

steps.title: Cook The Kale
steps.text: Destem and chop the Lacinato kale. Place a large nonstick skillet over medium- high heat with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the chopped kale, sesame seeds, and a pinch of salt and pepper, and cook until greens are just wilted, about 1 minute.

steps.title: Build Your Bowl
steps.text: To serve, scoop the quinoa speckled rice into large shallow bowls. Top with sambal cucumbers, sweet potato wedges, and sesame kale. Sprinkle with kizami nori and drizzle with yuzu sauce. Grab your chopsticks and enjoy!
