recipes.title: Autumn Quinoa Bowl
recipes.subtitle: with Brussels Slaw and Warm Coconut "Bacon"
recipes.summary: Coconut "bacon" is one of our recent favorites. We use just a bit of liquid smoke, maple, and miso to amp up the crispy texture of the coconut and add a savory twist. You can reuse the tahini dressing recipe for future greens and grains - it's easy to make and delicious.

steps.title: Cook The Quinoa
steps.text: Preheat oven to 300°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. In a small saucepan, combine the quinoa, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt and place over high heat. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook quinoa until water is absorbed and spirals start to burst, about 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Slaw
steps.text: Chop the walnuts. Place a small skillet over medium-high heat and add the walnuts. Cook until toasted, shaking the pan occasionally, about 3 to 5 minutes, and remove from heat. Thinly slice the Brussels sprouts, scallion, and apple and add to a large bowl along with the toasted walnuts and napa cabbage.

steps.title: Make The Coconut "Bacon"
steps.text: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. In a bowl, mix the miso paste, liquid smoke, a pinch of pepper, 1 tsp olive oil, and just 2 tsp maple syrup into a paste. Place the coconut chips on the baking sheet and toss them in the miso mixture, adding a little at a time until coated - you may not need all of the miso mixture. Bake until brown, stirring halfway to avoid sticking, about 5 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: Mix The Tahini Dressing
steps.text: In a small bowl, combine the tahini, apple cider vinegar, and remaining 1 tsp maple syrup. Add a pinch of salt and 3 tbsp water. Whisk tahini dressing until smooth.

steps.title: Toss The Slaw
steps.text: Add the tahini dressing to the Brussels slaw. Season with salt and pepper and toss slaw well to combine.

steps.title: Build Your Bowls
steps.text: Divide the cooked quinoa between large bowls. Top with Brussels slaw and warm coconut "bacon." Sprinkle with pomegranate seeds and dig in!

recipes.title: Creamy Mac n' Cheese
recipes.subtitle: with Green Chiles and Garlic Broccoli
recipes.summary: Creamy and decadent, no one will believe this mac n' cheese has a plant-based sauce! This family friendly meal can be made with or without the green chiles, which are generally on the milder side and packed with flavor. Buttery breadcrumbs coat the top and be sure to watch these closely in the oven, it should only take a few minutes. The garlic broccoli is a head nod to a childhood favorite; cooked last minute and served bright green.
ingredients: 8 oz broccoli florets recipe bag is for all recipes in the box this week.

steps.title: Set The Stage
steps.text: Place a large pot of salted water over high heat. Once boiling, add the pipette pasta, stir, and cook until tender, about 8 minutes. Drain but do not rinse. Mince the garlic.

steps.title: Start The Sauce
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Set aside 1 tsp of the minced garlic for Step 6. Once hot, add the remaining garlic and cook until slightly toasted, about 1 to 2 minutes. Add the arrowroot starch and whisk continuously for 1 minute.

steps.title: Make The Cheese Sauce
steps.text: Slowly whisk the almond milk into the large skillet and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring frequently. It's okay if this mixture is lumpy. Carefully transfer the sauce to a blender. Halve the lemon. Add juice from the lemon, dijon mustard, nutritional yeast, and vegan parmesan to the blender and season with salt and pepper. Blend, taste, and adjust seasoning with salt if necessary.

steps.title: Buttery Breadcrumbs
steps.text: Set oven to broil on high. Melt the vegan butter in a microwave or small skillet. In a small bowl, combine the melted butter, panko breadcrumbs, and a pinch of salt.

steps.title: Put It All Together
steps.text: Return the cheese sauce to the large skillet and place over medium-low heat. Add as much or as little of the green chiles as you'd like to the sauce. Bring sauce to a simmer and stir in cooked pasta. Transfer mac n' cheese to a 8x8 baking dish and top with buttery breadcrumbs. Broil until golden brown, about 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Serve It Up
steps.text: Rinse the large nonstick skillet and place over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Once hot, add the broccoli florets and reserved garlic. Cook until garlic is fragrant, about 1 minute. Add 2 tbsp water to create steam and cover. Cook until broccoli is just tender, about 4 to 5 minutes. Serve the creamy mac n' cheese on large plates with garlic broccoli. Dig in!

recipes.title: Refried Butternut Tacos
recipes.subtitle: with Roasted Grape Salsa and Plantains
recipes.summary: Our latest taco dish provides the perfect balance of sweet and spicy. Fragrant grapes and twice-cooked butternut squash complement flavorful chipotle and jalapeño. You'll enjoy your tacos with crispy plantains on the side.

steps.title: Cook The Butternut
steps.text: Preheat oven to 400°F. Add the butternut squash to a small saucepan and cover with at least 1 inch of cold water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and boil squash until tender (you should be able to pierce the flesh easily with a fork), about 11 to 13 minutes. Drain squash, return to the saucepan, and mash with a fork until smooth.

steps.title: Roast The Grapes
steps.text: Halve the grapes. Place on a baking sheet and toss with 1 tsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until grapes are soft, fragrant, and slightly wrinkled, about 6 to 8 minutes. Let cool for at least 5 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare The Salsa
steps.text: Deseed and mince the jalapeño. Thinly slice the scallion. Finely chop the cilantro leaves. Combine everything in a medium bowl and add the juice from half the lime. Once the grapes have cooled slightly, add to the bowl along with a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix salsa well to combine. Cut remaining lime half into wedges. Peel the plantain and slice into 1 inch thick slices on the diagonal.

steps.title: Fry The Butternut
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over mediumhigh heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the mashed butternut and press into a single layer in the skillet. Lower heat to medium and cook, undisturbed, until bottom is well browned, about 5 to 8 minutes. Stir, then smooth squash into an even layer once again, and cook for 5 minutes. Stir in just 1 tsp chipotle hot sauce, butter, and agave. Season with salt.

steps.title: Crisp The Plantains
steps.text: Wrap the flour tortillas in foil or a dish towel and warm them in the oven for about 5 minutes. Place a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once the oil is hot, add the sliced plantains and cook until browned on one side, about 3 to 4 minutes. Flip, sprinkle with salt, and cook until browned on the other side, another 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: Build Your Tacos
steps.text: Cut remaining lime half into wedges. Divide the refried butternut between the warm tortillas, spreading it out a bit. Add the roasted grape salsa and green cabbage. Drizzle on any remaining chipotle hot sauce and serve with crispy plantains and lime wedges. Dig in!
